meta.title: Frank-Frost Family Tree
meta.description: Genealogy database covering the Frost (maternal) and Frank/Lewis/Bickford/Fay (paternal) family lines, Whitney/Mosher lines (Kevin's maternal grandparents). Traces Canadian ancestry through the Fay family of Quebec. Also covers the Miller/Richards/Sacre lines (Pauline's family).

individuals.notes: Father of 7 children. Served in Civil War as cook's assistant. Military service began 30 April 1861 at Medford, age 41. Also enlisted 23 December 1863 at Sidney (Kennebec County). Second enlistment in Sidney, Kennebec County (1863) indicates return to his birth county after 1860. A Mark Frost (birth estimated 1819, age 58) appears in Maine Deaths and Burials dying 19 March 1877 in Augusta, Kennebec — consistent with age and county. Death date assigned medium-high confidence. 1870 CENSUS ANOMALY RESOLVED (session 3, 2026-03-31): The Rome, Kennebec Mark Frost with 'Lucy A Frost' (ark:/61903/1:1:M6D2-T6J) is a DIFFERENT person — FamilySearch lists separate Mark Frost persons with spouses 'Lucy A Frost' and 'Matilda Orcutt' in Kennebec County. Catherine Gibberd's FamilySearch profile (K6QM-FM6) records 1870 residence as Augusta, Kennebec (0 sources) — she was likely at the Maine Insane Hospital by this date, consistent with her confirmed 1900 residence there. Our Mark Frost was likely also in the Augusta area near Catherine's institution, and died there 19 March 1877. This resolution elevates death record confidence from 'medium' to 'medium-high'. FamilySearch PID: GQ4X-WZ1.
individuals.source_citations: William H. Frost death certificate; FindAGrave Catherine E. Gibberd Frost memorial #113000115; FamilySearch GQ4X-WZ1; Maine Births and Christenings 1739-1900 (ark:/61903/1:1:F44Z-6JQ); Maine Civil War Soldiers and Sailors Card Index 1861-1865 (ark:/61903/1:1:246N-2XK); Maine Deaths and Burials 1841-1910 — Mark Frost d.19 Mar 1877 Augusta (ark:/61903/1:1:F48P-2M6) [medium confidence]; 1870 US Census — Mark Frost, Rome, Kennebec, ME (ark:/61903/1:1:M6D2-T6J) [uncertain identity]

individuals.notes: Child of Mark and Catherine Frost. FamilySearch PID: LTMR-ZQ5. Resided: 1850 Kilmarnock, ME; 1860 Medford, Piscataquis, ME (confirmed in 1860 census). SESSION 3 FINDING (2026-03-31): Found in 1865 Massachusetts State Census (ark:/61903/1:1:MQH5-3Q1) in Haverhill, Essex County, MA — age 17, born 1848 Maine, single, living in household of Nathaniel Frost (age 61, born 1804 Maine, widowed). Nathaniel Frost (PID M665-WYH, 1804-1879) is confirmed as Benjamin Frost's 8th child — Mark Frost's older brother and Sarah's uncle. Sarah left Maine as a teenager to live with extended family in Massachusetts, paralleling sister Nellie Burton Frost who also moved to MA young. The 1865 record is linked to her 1860 census entry via FamilySearch 'Similar Records', confirming identity. Other household members: Nathaniel Frost Jr (35), Loring W Frost (27), Helen Frost (20, NH), Lorinda Frost (38), Caroline A Frost (30), Fidelia Frost (28), Ellen L Frost (19). Also noted: NJ marriage record (ark:/61903/1:1:FZGK-VQ1) shows a 'Sarah F. Frost' (age 42, birth ~1847, U.S.) marrying Robert Waldron on 1 Oct 1889 in Rahway, Union, NJ — low confidence match (birth year close but no state-level birthplace confirmation; listed as previously married). ELIMINATED: 1880 Census Hampden, Penobscot 'Sarah F. Frost' (ark:/61903/1:1:MFQM-549) married to 'Chas. Frost' — this woman's maiden name was Sara Freeman Gorton per Maine Vital Records and FindAGrave; she is a different person. No records after 1865 confirmed for our Sarah.
individuals.source_citations: Catherine Gibberd Frost FindAGrave bio; FamilySearch LTMR-ZQ5; 1865 Massachusetts State Census (ark:/61903/1:1:MQH5-3Q1) — Sarah F Frost, age 17, Haverhill, Essex, MA [medium confidence]; 1860 US Census Medford, Piscataquis (ark:/61903/1:1:MDHT-MZ2)

individuals.notes: Sibling of William and John. FamilySearch PID: GQ4X-RRV.

Married name Hubbard. Married Philander L Hubbard (ID 148, 1831-1906) ~1870, Fairfield, Somerset, ME. Five children: Cora E Hubbard (1871-1898), Lenora F Hubbard (1873-1930), Wilton Sherman Hubbard (1876-1950), Wilbur Hubbard (1876-Deceased), Ernest Mark Hubbard (1877-Deceased). Child named Mark E Hubbard in 1880 census — named after Clara's father Mark Frost. Family moved: Fairfield Somerset ME → Chapman Aroostook ME (1880) → Bradford/Bangor Penobscot ME (1900-1930). Burial per FamilySearch: Williams Cemetery, Bradford, Penobscot, ME (near husband Philander). FamilySearch PID: GQ4F-7LH (13 sources). SESSION 3 FINDINGS (2026-03-31): 1900 Census (ark:/61903/1:1:MMLR-SV4): Clara R Hubbard, age 47, born Aug 1853, Maine, married 30 years, 5 children total / 3 living, listed as 'Sister' of household head John L Frost (age 45) — her brother John Lyman Frost (ID 7). Father's birthplace: Maine (=Mark Frost), Mother's birthplace: Maine (=Catherine Gibberd). Confirms Clara is daughter of Mark and Catherine Frost. 1930 Census (ark:/61903/1:1:XMD7-PCS): Clara R Hubbard, age 75, widowed, Bradford, Penobscot, ME. Listed as 'mother-in-law' of household head Fred L Jones (55). Son Ernest M Hubbard (52) also in household. Not found in 1940 census — died between April 1930 and April 1940 (age ~77-87). Death date remains unknown. Neither FindAGrave nor Nathan Hale Cemetery Collection record a death date. Residences: 1850 Norway, Oxford, ME; 1860 Medford, Piscataquis, ME; 1880 Chapman, Aroostook, ME; 1900 Bangor Ward 1, Penobscot, ME; 1910-1930 Bradford, Penobscot, ME.

Middle initial 'G' matches Lewis G Frank; age 5 in May 1950 is consistent with birth Jul 1944. 'Davis' may be a census transcription error or an unused given name.

individuals.notes: Part of the German Storer family of Waldoboro. Parents: Christian David Storer (KG6R-3FJ, 1752-1827) and Catharine Mary Heavener (KG6R-3NJ, 1758-1824). The Storer family arrived in Broad Bay (later Waldoboro) from Germany circa 1752. Andrew George was one of 10 children. Married Mary Mink Keizer 16 Nov 1809 in Waldoboro. Had 7 children. Died age 53 in New Vineyard, Franklin County — apparently moved west within Maine. Son Andrew Obed was 19 when father died.

individuals.notes: Born into the Keizer family of Waldoboro, another German immigrant family. Parents: Francis Keizer (K8SC-B2F, 1747-1790) and Catherine Gerdraut Mink (K2XS-H3R, 1753-1846). Mary was one of 7 children. Middle name "Mink" is her mother's maiden name. Husband Andrew George died 1831 when Mary was ~42. She survived him by 28 years, dying in 1859. By 1850 still living in Waldoboro. Her son Andrew Obed had moved to Bradford by then.

birth.place: Bridgeport, Logan Township, Gloucester County, NJ

individuals.notes: Kevin's paternal grandfather. Full name: Joseph Franklin Frank. Christened 12 Apr 1925, Bridgeport, Logan Township, Gloucester, NJ. Youngest of 10 children of William Biddle Frank and Mary E. Farnkopf. Married Mildred Elsie Lewis. Father died when Joseph was 4 years old. Occupation: Operator (per 1950 census). Race: White. Buried in Gloucester County, NJ (cemetery TBD). FamilySearch PID: GDT8-FWZ. NJ Death Index: State File 20100066448. 9 siblings: William T (1896-1974), Laura (1898-1898), Augustus (1899-1986), Harry D (1902-1992), Harry H (1903-1992), George Reed (1903-1988), Anna Elizabeth Croft (1905-1992), Warren DuBois (1909-1966), Albert Jacob (1912-2001). 1940 census: married with infant daughter Mildred E (age 0) in Logan Township; Mildred wife listed as age 21 (born ~1918-1919, consistent with actual birth July 1918). Residence in 1935 was same house (Logan Township). Marriage likely ~1938-1939. 1950 census: 4 children listed — Mildred E (daughter, 10), Thomas W (son), Joseph D (son, 8), Davis G (son, 5). RESOLVED: 'Davis G Frank, son, age 5' in 1950 census IS Lewis G Frank (born 1944-07-15, age 5 on May 18, 1950). 'Davis' was a transcription error of 'Lewis'; the 'G' middle initial matches. Confirmed by Kevin Frank. Known children (from 1940/1950 census): Mildred E (~1940, ID 134), Thomas W (~1941, ID 135), Joseph D (~1942, ID 136), Lewis (1944, ID 22), and possibly Davis G (~1945, identity unclear). 1920 census (age 4, 'Joseph F Frank') in William B Frank household, Gloucester, NJ (Source 212). Mother's 1956 obituary confirms 'Joseph, of Swedesboro' as one of seven surviving sons. 1950 census (ark:/61903/1:1:6F9P-64PY) shows household: Joseph L Frank (head, age 34, operator, chemical plant), wife Mildred E Frank (age 31), daughter Mildred E (age 10), son Joseph D (age 8), son Davis G (age 5), son Thomas W. 'Davis G Frank' (age 5) is likely Lewis G Frank — matching middle initial and birth year (~1944-45). CHRISTENING: 12 Apr 1925 at Bridgeport, Logan Township, NJ (same date as brothers Warren and Albert — batch christening). FamilySearch PID: GDT8-FWZ. 1940 CENSUS (Source 225): Head of household at Bridgeport, Logan Township, age 24, married, born NJ. Wife E Mildred Frank age 21. Daughter M Mildred Frank age 0. Residence in 1935: same house.

individuals.notes: Kevin's paternal grandmother. Maiden name Lewis. Family from Vermont with property in Barton, Orleans County, VT. NJ Death Index: State File 20070012676. Possible connection to Bert E Lewis family of Barton/Newport, VT. Canadian ancestry suspected (Nova Scotia/Quebec border area). Full name: Mildred Elsie Lewis. FamilySearch GPZ3-8HB. Parents: Roswell Eldridge Lewis (M46N-HXT) and Muriel Helena Bickford (KGNR-86G). Married Joseph Franklin Frank. AKA Mildred E Frank. Vermont connection through mother (Bickford family of Barton, VT). Canadian connection through maternal great-grandfather Michael L Fay (b. Saint-Armand, Quebec). FamilySearch PID: GPZ3-8HB. Birthplace confirmed as Clarksboro, East Greenwich Township (not just Gloucester County). Parents per FamilySearch tree: Roswell Eldridge Lewis (M46N-HXT, 1891-1968) and Muriel Helena Bickford (KGNR-86G, 1891-1962), married 6 Mar 1914 in Bucks County, PA. individuals.notes: Mother of Roswell Eldridge Lewis. Born 31 Oct 1867 in Goodhue, Minnesota (per FamilySearch). Census records list birthplace variously as New York (1900) and South Dakota (1910). EXPLANATION: Booth family lived in Burnside Township, Goodhue County, MN until ~1870-1880, then migrated to Sioux Falls, Dakota Territory. May was born in MN (1867) but grew up in SD — likely accounts for South Dakota birthplace claims in later censuses. May is ABSENT from 1880 Sioux Falls household (would be age ~12-13); may have been elsewhere at time of census or missed in indexing. Married William H Lewis in 1887. Widowed after William's death ~1925. Lived in East Greenwich Twp, Gloucester, NJ from ~1915 until death. Buried in Clarksboro, NJ — same town where granddaughter Mildred was born. FamilySearch PID: GXTR-LQZ. 1870 Census (ark:/61903/1:1:MDHS-8LH): May Booth, age 2, female, Minnesota, in Burnside Township, Goodhue, MN. Household head Richard H Booth (age 43, NY) and wife Sarah Booth (age 41, NY). Siblings in 1870: Kate (20), Richard (13), Carrie (11), Fred (9), Ida (7), Alice (3), Charlotte (0), Franklin (7), Irving (4). HIGH confidence parents: Richard H Booth (ID 141) and Sarah C Booth (ID 142) — confirmed by location match, FamilySearch birth record, and cross-reference with 1870/1880/1900 censuses. Updated 2026-03-31 Session 4.

individuals.notes: Beryl's mother. Full name Violet Dorothy Sacre. Born Q3 1908 (Jul-Aug-Sep), Woolwich registration district, London. Maiden name Sacre — a Norman-French surname. Family memory records her as 'originally French, emigrated to England' but GRO birth index shows registration in Woolwich, London. The French connection likely refers to the family's ancestral origins rather than Violet's birthplace. Married William Joshua M Richards Q1 1930 in Lambeth, London. Family lived in south London (Woolwich, Camberwell, Southwark) 1930s, then moved to Sturminster, Dorset ~1940-42 (possibly WWII evacuation). At least 8 children. Resided Sturminster area of Dorset by 1942. Death date 10 October 1982 per WikiTree genealogy database (source 103). Birth date of 15 June 1908 per WikiTree — note possible conflict with GRO Q3 (Jul-Sep) 1908 registration; registration may have been delayed. Full parents now identified: father Edwin William G Sacre (ID 119), mother Edith Mary Ellis née Ellis (ID 120).

individuals.notes: Beryl's father. Full name William Joshua M Richards (third given name initial M, full name unknown). Born 7 May 1902, registered Q2 1902 in Lambeth, London (Vol 1D, p 331). Married Violet Dorothy Sacre Q1 1930 in Lambeth. Died September 1988, Greenwich, London. Family lived in south London (Woolwich, Camberwell, Southwark) during the 1930s, then relocated to Sturminster, Dorset ~1940-42 (possibly WWII-related). At least 8 children born 1931-1950. Family may have returned to London area after the war (youngest child Janet born London 1950). Died in Greenwich, suggesting he lived in south London in later life. Henry Richards (d. 1954) and Gertrude Richards (d. 1978) buried in Sturminster Newton Cemetery — possibly William's parents or relatives who remained in Dorset. Parents identified: William Richards (ID 183, b. ~1865, Lambeth, carman) and Sarah Richards (ID 184, b. ~1865, Lambeth). Identification based on 1911 census showing a 'William Richards, Son, M, 9, born Lambeth' in household of William and Sarah Richards in Lambeth — matching the only William Richards born Q2 1902 in Lambeth (William Joshua M). 1901 census of same household shows older brother Henry Richards (age 11), who later settled in Sturminster Newton, Dorset (d. 1954, FindAGrave #192271800). Henry's presence in Dorset likely drew William's family to evacuate there from London during WWII (~1940-1942). 1939 Register (Findmypast): William Richards, birth year 1902, residing at 196 Quinn Square, Waterloo, Lambeth, borough of Southwark, London. This confirms his south London residence immediately before WWII, and is consistent with the family relocating to Sturminster, Dorset ~1940-42.

individuals.notes: Kevin's paternal great-great-grandfather. Born 22 Mar 1824 in Nordheim, Württemberg, Germany. Christened 25 Mar 1824 in Nordheim. Died 2 Nov 1889 in Logan Township, Gloucester, NJ. German immigrant. Married Mary Magdeline Bundes (ID 71). 7 children. Parents: Johannes Franck (ID 162, 1788–1853) and Christine Friederike Rudolph (ID 163, 1787–1857), married 25 Nov 1817. Jacob was 4th of 7 children. FamilySearch PID: KZ2R-8GZ. Also listed as 'Jacob Franks' and 'Jacob Frank' in American records. OCCUPATION: Farmer (confirmed by 1853 Germans to America Index and 1880 US Census). IMMIGRATION: Probable arrival 30 April 1853 via ship Persian departing Havre (Le Havre, France). Father Johannes died 18 Feb 1853 — Jacob may have emigrated shortly after. RESIDENCE HISTORY: 1860 census — Woolwich Township, Gloucester, NJ; 1880 census — Upper Penns Neck, Salem, NJ (adjacent to Logan Township area); death 1889 — Logan Township, Gloucester, NJ. Confirmed burial: Bridgeport Cemetery (Saint Pauls Cemetery), Bridgeport, Gloucester County, NJ (FindAGrave #118358287). HEADSTONE NOTE: Grave headstone records birth year as 1823; christening record (more authoritative) gives 22 Mar 1824. CAROLINE FRANK NOTE: 1860 census does NOT show Caroline in the household — she would have been ~4 if born 1856. Either died before June 1860 or birth year is incorrect. FamilySearch lists 0 sources for Caroline (G6GK-939). 1870 CENSUS: Not yet found in indexed records. May be unindexed or surname variant not surfacing.

individuals.immigration: Emigrated from Germany to New Jersey (date unknown; before first NJ child born ~1856-1857)
individuals.notes: Kevin's paternal great-great-grandmother. Married Jacob Friedrich Franck (ID 70). 7 children: Caroline (1856), Frederic (1857), Charles G (1858), Minnie (1860), Elizabeth (1861), Kate (1865), William B (1869). Also listed as 'Mary Magdeline Bundes Franks'. FamilySearch PID: G6GV-X2D. CONFIRMED: Born in Germany (not New Jersey) — 1860 US Census shows birthplace as Germany. BIRTH YEAR CONFLICT: FamilySearch profile records birth year 1837; however, the 1860 census records her age as 28, implying birth year ~1832. This 5-year discrepancy requires further investigation. The 1837 date on FamilySearch appears to be unsourced user contribution. BUNDES SURNAME: The surname 'Bundes' does not appear in any US genealogical records indexed on FamilySearch, suggesting the name may have been preserved through family oral tradition. The surname is concentrated in the Rømø / Schleswig-Holstein / northern Germany region in European records, suggesting Mary's origin may be northern Germany or the Danish-German border region — geographically different from Jacob's Württemberg origin. They likely met in New Jersey after both emigrated separately. RESIDENCE: 1860 census places family in Woolwich Township, Gloucester, NJ. Family moved to Logan Township (Bridgeport area) before Jacob's death in 1889. Mary died 2 March 1910 in Logan Township, Gloucester, NJ. Burial presumed at Bridgeport Cemetery (Saint Pauls Cemetery), but no FindAGrave memorial found. NOTE: Caroline (birth 1856) is absent from the 1860 census — either died before June 1860, or the 1856 birth year in the FamilySearch tree is incorrect.

individuals.notes: Bernice F Bailey's father. John S Miller's maternal grandfather. First wife: Elizabeth Malanoski (m. 14 May 1906, died April 1924). Second wife: Elizabeth M [maiden name unknown] Bialy (1889-1981), buried together at Saint James Cemetery, Waverly, NY (FindAGrave #145177201). 8 children by first wife: Harriett T, Jadwiga, Harriet, Bernice F, Marie, Alexander, Helen, Margaret. individuals.notes: Elizabeth Malanoski Bailey's father. Born 25 March 1866 in Żałe, Brzuze, radziejowski county, Kuyavian-Pomeranian Voivodeship, Poland. Christened 28 March 1866 in same village. Birth name: Marian Malanowski (Polish form). Immigrated to the United States in 1888. Settled in Elmira, Chemung County, New York. Residences: 1900 Elmira Ward 6, Chemung, NY; 1905 Elmira Ward 06, Chemung, NY. Died 1929 in Elmira, NY. First wife: Leokadia Rogowski (1866-1919), married 1884 in Poland, 12 children. Second wife: Johanna Nowakowski (1889-deceased, FamilySearch LTKQ-DJ1), married 6 Sep 1919, New York, 2 children. Parents: Konstanty Malanowski (1835-1902, FamilySearch GSMB-ML1) and Wiktoria Cetkowska (1840-1903, FamilySearch GSMB-3ZL), married 1860 in Żałe. One of 6 siblings. FamilySearch PID: LTKQ-9YB (13 sources). Parents confirmed from FamilySearch: father Konstanty Malanowski (GSMB-ML1, born 1835 Poland, died 1902 Poland, now ID 132 in tree) and mother Wiktoria Cetkowska (GSMB-3ZL, born 1840 Poland, died 1903 Poland, now ID 133 in tree). Death date refined to 29 April 1929 from NY State Death Index entry 25129. Michael remarried 6 Sep 1919 to Johanna Novakowski (FamilySearch LTKQ-DJ1) — only ~4 months after Leokadia's death. Had 2 more children with Johanna.

individuals.notes: Elizabeth Malanoski Bailey's mother. Born December 1866 in Russian-controlled Poland (parents' birthplace also listed as Poland Russia per 1900 US Census; specific parish unknown). Married Michael Marian Malanoski in 1884 in Poland (date per FamilySearch sources; 1900 census lists 14 years married, suggesting ~1886 — discrepancy noted). Immigrated to the United States in 1888. Settled in Elmira, Chemung County, New York. Residences: 1900 Elmira Ward 6, Chemung, NY (as 'Eliza Malanosky', age 34, birth Dec 1866, Poland Russia); 1905 Elmira Ward 06, Chemung, NY. Died 19 May 1919 in Elmira, NY (NY State Death Index entry 29716). Buried at Saint Peter and Paul Cemetery, Elmira, Chemung County, NY (FindAGrave memorial #151995906, confirmed 2026-03-31). Her death preceded her husband's second marriage (6 Sep 1919) by approximately 108 days. 12 children with Michael Malanoski: Elizabeth (1886), Jazeski (1888, died in infancy), Malanoski (1890, died in infancy), John Joseph (1891-1967), Zygmunt Stanislaus (1895-1987), Bernard Joe (1896-1964), Felix (1899-deceased), Robert Leo Lady Slaw (1901-1966), Wadlich (1902-deceased), Thomas J. (1903-1970), Stella V. (1907-1981), Stanislaus (1907, died in infancy). FamilySearch PID: GQV3-TQ7 (12 sources). Funeral notice in Elmira Star-Gazette 22 May 1919: service at family home, 1010 North Main Street, Elmira, and at St. Casimir's Polish Catholic Church at 9 a.m. Rev. Joseph A. Balcerak officiated requiem high mass. Pallbearers: Paul Mecraes, Vincent Sobkowski, John Gosiezki, Peter Scarveski, Jacob Droleski, William Schrienski. FamilySearch profile (GQV3-TQ7) confirms no parents listed — Rogowski Polish origins remain unresearched. MARRIAGE DATE CONFLICT: FamilySearch sources give 1884 for Poland marriage; 1900 US Census '14 years married' implies ~1886. The Polish record (1884) is likely more reliable than the census estimate.

individuals.residence: Canterbury, Kent (born 1885); Charlton and Kidbrooke, London (1901); Woolwich, London (1962 death)
individuals.notes: Violet Dorothy Sacre's mother. Maiden name Ellis. Married Edwin William G Sacre Q1 1904 in Woolwich (GRO Vol. 1D, p. 1366). FamilySearch PID GDNK-3SK. Birth date: FamilySearch ancestors page gives 31 July 1885; however the GRO birth registration is in Q4 1885 (Oct-Nov-Dec), which conflicts with a July 31 birth (which should register in Q3). The birth date of 31 July may be from the 1911 census and could be approximate, or the registration was significantly delayed. Using 1885 as confirmed birth year; exact date to be verified against GRO certificate. Born in Canterbury, Kent. 1901 census: Found as 'Edith M Ellis', age 15, born Canterbury, Kent, living in Charlton and Kidbrooke (Woolwich registration district) — same area Edwin W G Sacre lived. Siblings in household: Richard W Ellis (13, Dover, Kent), Arthur E Ellis (11, Charlton), Samuel J Ellis (8, Charlton). This confirms she was in Woolwich area before her 1904 marriage to Edwin Sacre. PARENTS CONFIRMED (session 14): Father: Richard Ellis (ID 130), born ~1850 Scotland, Clerk at Royal Arsenal Woolwich. Mother: Annie Ellis (ID 131), born ~1851 Farnham, Surrey. Confirmed via 1891 census (household 975230, Charlton Next Woolwich) showing 'Edith M Ellis, Daughter, age 5, Kent' with Richard Ellis (Head, Scotland) and Annie Ellis (Wife, Surrey). individuals.notes: Biological mother of Edwin William G Sacre (ID 119). FamilySearch PID LB44-YCB. Born 1860 in Erith, Kent. Their only known child: Edwin William G Sacre (born October 1881, Kidbrooke, London). Harriet died October 1889 in Woolwich at age ~29, when Edwin W G was only ~8 years old. Following her death, Edwin William Sacre remarried Ann Alice (~1892). Harriet's parents Thomas Gurr and Harriet Ann Benge are documented on her FamilySearch profile but not yet added to this tree BIRTH DATE CORRECTION (session 16, 2026-03-30): The England Births and Christenings record (FamilySearch ark:/61903/1:1:J39G-3QY) gives christening date 2 October 1859, Erith, Kent — NOT 1860 as shown in the FamilySearch Family Tree profile (LB44-YCB). The christening record clearly states 'Event Date: 2 Oct 1859' and 'Event Place: Erith, Kent, England'. Birth year is therefore 1859. The 1860 date in the FamilySearch profile appears to be an error. Birth date updated from '1860' to '~1859-10' (christening 2 Oct 1859, birth likely a few weeks prior).

individuals.notes: Father of Edith Mary Ellis (ID 120) and her siblings. Husband of Annie Ellis (ID 131). Born ~1850 in Scotland (per 1891 census). Occupation: Clerk at the Royal Arsenal, Woolwich (1891 census). Head of household in Charlton Next Woolwich in 1891 (household 975230) and 1901 (household 994159). Children at time of 1891 census: Ernest (Arthur Ernest, age 1), Emma (age 10, born Singapore), Richard (older son, age 13, born Kent), Edith M (age 5, born Kent). Children confirmed in 1901 census: Edith M (15, Canterbury), Richard W (13, Dover), Arthur E (11, Charlton), Samuel J (8, Charlton). Richard's death occurred between 31 March 1901 (when he is listed as head/husband in the 1901 census) and April 1911 (when Annie is listed as widowed). The 1901 household search in session 13 failed because Richard was born in Scotland, outside the expected birth range, and his record was not well-indexed under location-based searches. His presence in the 1901 household is confirmed via Annie Ellis's 1901 census record (wife, married, household 994159). Richard worked at the Royal Arsenal — the major government armaments facility in Woolwich — which explains the family's long residence in the Charlton/Woolwich area. The family also appears to have spent time in Singapore (daughter Emma born there ~1881) and moved through Canterbury and Dover before settling in Charlton by ~1890.

individuals.interesting_facts: Born in Scotland but spent career as a Clerk at the Royal Arsenal in Woolwich, England. The family appears to have lived in Singapore for a period (daughter Emma born there ~1881), possibly related to Richard's work or military connections.

individuals.military_service: Served in the US military; lost his eyesight during military service (per family knowledge).
individuals.notes: Son of Joseph Franklin Frank and Mildred Elsie Lewis Frank. Full name: Thomas William Frank. Kevin's paternal uncle ("Uncle Tom"). Born 15 December 1949 in New Jersey. Served in the US military, where he went blind during service (per family knowledge from Kevin). Moved to Vermont. Worked for the State of Vermont for many years. Lives in Milton, Chittenden County, Vermont. Associated with the Vermont Division for the Blind and Visually Impaired. Married (1) Lucy Ely Pagán (ID 137, from Puerto Rico) — sons Brian Thomas Frank (born 16 Mar 1984, St. Johnsbury, VT, ID 139) and Michael Steven Frank (Waterbury Center, VT, ID 138). Thomas and Lucy appear separated (different residences as of wedding announcement). Married (2) Janet Ann Dooley (born 21 Jul 1947) on 18 Aug 2007 in Barton, Orleans, VT. Appears in 1950 census (May 1950) as "Thomas W Frank, son" in Joseph's household — he was 5 months old. Vermont marriage certificate (2007) lists parents as "Joseph" and "Mildred", confirming parentage. Note: A VT vital record for "Thomas Willy Frank / Louise Francine Frank / Paul Michael Frank" is a DIFFERENT Thomas Frank — not this person.

individuals.notes: Fourth child of William Henry Frost and Esther M Thompson. Died young at 35 in Oakland, Kennebec, ME. Married Mary A Willette/Wann (1880-1965, PID L1FZ-FPG), 14 May 1911, Oakland, Kennebec County, ME. Three children: Angelia Estella Frost (1912-2009, L1FZ-RPX), Clarence Russell Frost (1913-1985, L1FZ-595), Donald Raymond Frost (1918-Deceased, L1FZ-Y1M). FamilySearch PID: L1FZ-DFZ (12 sources). SESSION 3 FINDINGS (2026-03-31): Death record confirmed: Maine Vital Records 1670-1921 (ark:/61903/1:1:Q24F-TMG8) — Winfield C Frost, Male, age 35, death 6 October 1919, Oakland, Kennebec, Maine. Cause of death not available in indexed record — would require viewing original death certificate image (microfilm 000010117, image 2680). Context: October 1919 falls during the Spanish Flu pandemic's third wave; death at 35 could be flu-related but this is speculative. Marriage record (ark:/61903/1:1:KCN5-QLK): Winfield C Frost married Mary A Wann, 14 May 1911, Oakland, ME. Both resided in Oakland. Second marriage source (Maine Vital Records ark:/61903/1:1:KCNY-H67 via search): confirms parents as Wm Frost and Esther M Thompson. Wife also appears as 'Mary Willette' and 'Mary A Willett' in some records — Wann appears to be maiden name, Willette/Willett may be variant. Cemetery burial: Oakland, Kennebec, ME per Nathan Hale Cemetery Collection (ark:/61903/1:1:QVJ5-WMBJ, listed as 'Winfield G Frost' — G is transcription error for C).

FamilySearch PID: KHP1-Q73 (34 sources). His own parents: Jacob Friederich Franck (1764–1832, 97LY-JVS) and Anna Margaretha Mack (1764–1834, MRBZ-6BF), married 21 Nov 1785.
individuals.interesting_facts: Father of the immigrant Jacob Friedrich Franck (ID 70). Died in Klingenberg — same village where his wife Christine died 4 years later in 1857. Jacob likely emigrated to America shortly after his father's death in February 1853 (arrived April 1853).

NOTE — Name: FindAGrave and Mattawamkeag Cemetery headstone both use 'John Adams Hathaway' (or 'John A. Hathaway'). FamilySearch PID LC11-MNC was previously noted as 'John Quincy Adams Hathaway' — possible middle name error in FamilySearch tree; retained as alias pending verification.

NOTE — Birth year conflict: FindAGrave lists birth as 20 Feb 1850; the Mattawamkeag Cemetery headstone transcript (penobscotcountymegen.info) gives 1851. Father Justus's memorial bio independently states 'JOHN ADAMS HATHAWAY, b. 20 Feb 1850' — this corroborates the FindAGrave date. Using 1850-02-20.

FamilySearch PID: LC11-MNC (from prior research; unverified in this session due to login).

Interesting: Adelaide S. Hathaway (John's sister, b.1844) married John Otis Hale — the brother of John Adams's own wife Orissa Jane Hale. Two Hathaway siblings married two Hale siblings.

individuals.residence: 1880 Hector, Schuyler County, NY (single, farmer); 1900 Burdett, Schuyler, NY; 1920 Waverly, Tioga, NY (widowed)
individuals.notes: Robert L Miller's father. Pauline's paternal great-great-grandfather. FamilySearch PID: GS5Y-5KH. Full name Franklin J Miller (from NUMIDENT, ark:/61903/1:1:6K4J-YS7W). Head of household in 1900 US Census, Burdett, Hector Township, ED 50, Schuyler County, NY (household 277). Wife: Anna Everetts (born March 1857 NY). Two sons listed in 1900 census: Robert (born May 1883) and Earnest (born ~1886). In 1920 US Census (ark:/61903/1:1:MVSP-S5M): living in Waverly, Barton, Tioga County, NY, listed as widowed. Father's birthplace: New York. Mother's birthplace: New York. Occupation: farmer (based on rural Hector Township location in 1900). Anna died between 1900 and 1920 (Frank listed as widowed in 1920 census). 1880 US Census (Source 206): Frank Miller, age 24, single, farmer, head of household, Hector, District 3, Schuyler County, NY. Father born New York, mother born Delaware. Living with Mary Miller, F, 54, widowed, born Delaware, relationship "Other" — possibly his mother. CONFLICT: NUMIDENT (Source 204) names mother as "Lucy"; 1920 census (Source 203) says mother born NY; 1880 census says mother born Delaware. The 1880 Mary Miller may or may not be his mother — she is listed as "Other" not "Mother" in the relationship field. If Mary is his mother, her name discrepancy with "Lucy" could indicate: (a) her full name was Mary Lucy or Lucy Mary, (b) NUMIDENT was inaccurate (Frank was ~80 when he applied), or (c) Mary was not his mother. 1860 Census (Source 235): John F Miller, age 5, born ~1855, New York, in household of Mary Miller (age 45, Connecticut), William Miller (age 36, NY), Emmeline Miller (19, NY), Harrison Miller (17, NY), Mary L Miller (8, NY), Catharine, Schuyler County, NY (ark:/61903/1:1:MC4D-H6J). "John F" likely = Franklin ("Frank" / "John Franklin"). Catharine township is adjacent to Hector in Schuyler County. CONFLICT: This 1860 household head is Mary (45, born CT), not Robert — NUMIDENT names father as Robert Miller. William Miller (36) in household could be an uncle, older brother, or other relation.

individuals.residence: 1881: St George The Martyr Southwark (with parents); 1891: St George The Martyr Southwark (with parents, age 22); 1901: Lambeth, London; 1911: Lambeth, London
individuals.notes: Father of William Joshua M Richards (ID 60). Identified through 1901 and 1911 census records. Age 36 in 1901 (b. ~1865), age 44 in 1911 (b. ~1867) — typical census age discrepancy. Born Lambeth, London. Occupation: carman (horse-drawn cart driver), industry listed as 'rubbish contractor' in 1911. FamilySearch tree PID: GNGZ-YT9. Family lived in Lambeth (Kennington/Vauxhall SE area) through at least 1911. Parents identified via 1881 census (Source 224): father William Richards (ID 191, tinman, b. ~1839 Southwark) and mother Mary Ann Richards (ID 192, b. ~1843 Lambeth). In 1881, William was age 15, a scholar, living at St George The Martyr Southwark with parents and 5 siblings: Mary (16), Eliza (13), Emma (8), James (4), Betsy (2). FamilySearch tree links: this William is GT3T-MSY, father LXQS-P1Y, mother LXQS-SM5. 1891 CENSUS (Source 229): Still living in parents' household at St George The Martyr Southwark, listed as 'William Richards, Son, M, 22 years, London, England.' No wife or children present — appears to have been unmarried in April 1891. This means he married Sarah Parsons AFTER the 1891 census, likely in Q2 1893 (Source 230). Age 22 in 1891 gives birth ~1869, vs age 15 in 1881 (born ~1866), age 36 in 1901 (born ~1865), age 44 in 1911 (born ~1867) — typical census age variation, suggesting actual birth ~1865-1869. MARRIAGE RECORD FOUND (Source 230): William Richards married Sarah Parsons in Lambeth, Q2 1893 (Apr-May-Jun), Vol 1D, p 709. Cross-referenced via Sarah Parsons entry at same Vol/Page/Quarter (ark:/61903/1:1:2DFN-9DZ). This reveals Sarah's maiden name as PARSONS.

individuals.residence: Lambeth, London (1891, 1901, 1911); born St. Saviour Southwark
individuals.notes: Mother of William Joshua M Richards (ID 60). Identified through 1901 and 1911 census records. Age 36 in 1901 (b. ~1865), age 46 in 1911 (b. ~1865). BIRTH REGISTRATION FOUND: Sarah J Parsons, Q4 1866, St. Saviour Southwark, Vol 1D, Page 13 (Source 236). This is the SAME registration district where the Richards tinman family (IDs 191, 192) lived in 1881, 1891, and ~1899 — strongly suggesting the Parsons and Richards families were neighbors in Southwark. MAIDEN NAME IDENTIFIED: Parsons. Married William Richards in Lambeth, Q2 1893 (Apr-May-Jun), Vol 1D, p 709 (Source 230). 1891 CENSUS: Sarah Parsons, age 25, WIDOWED, Washer, Head of Household, Lambeth, with son Henry Parsons age 1 (Source 237). The 'widowed' status is likely a social convention to avoid stigma of illegitimacy — no marriage record for Sarah to anyone before the 1893 marriage to William Richards has been found in the GRO Marriage Registration Index. Henry (born Q2 1890 as Henry Hector Parsons) predates the Q2 1893 marriage by ~3 years. FamilySearch tree PID 9WF8-QGF (separate from the Richards tree entry) shows Sarah with spouse 'Henry Parsons' (9WF8-QG8) — this is likely an error; no corroborating marriage record found. Birth year discrepancy: GRO says 1866, 1891 census says ~1866 (age 25), 1901 census says ~1865 (age 36), 1911 census says ~1865 (age 46). The GRO birth index is the most reliable; the 1901/1911 ages are ~1 year off.
individuals.source_citations: 1901 Census (Source 213, ark:/61903/1:1:X9H6-B2B); 1911 Census (Source 214, ark:/61903/1:1:XWP1-G55); Marriage Registration Index Q2 1893 Lambeth (Source 230, ark:/61903/1:1:2DFN-9DZ); GRO Birth Registration Index Q4 1866 St. Saviour Southwark (Source 236, ark:/61903/1:1:2XSC-VDZ); 1891 Census Lambeth (Source 237, ark:/61903/1:1:QC6P-BT2)

individuals.burial_place: Sturminster Newton Cemetery, North Dorset, Dorset, England (FindAGrave #192271800)
individuals.notes: Older brother of William Joshua M Richards (ID 60). BIRTH NAME: Henry Hector Parsons. Birth registered Q2 1890 (Apr-May-Jun), Lambeth, Vol 1D, Page 390 (Source 238). Registered under mother's maiden name Parsons — born ~3 years BEFORE parents' Q2 1893 marriage. 1891 Census: 'Henry Parsons, Son, M, 1 year' in household of Sarah Parsons (widowed, age 25) in Lambeth (Source 237). After mother married William Richards in 1893, Henry took the Richards surname. Listed as 'Henry Richards, Son, M, 11' in 1901 census (Lambeth). Not present in 1911 census household (aged ~21, moved out). Married Gertrude (ID 186). Buried at Sturminster Newton Cemetery, Dorset. FamilySearch tree PID for birth record: 9WF8-QLG (as Henry Hector Parsons). Middle name 'Hector' from GRO birth index — not used in later life (all census records show just 'Henry'). Age discrepancy: 1901 census age 11 (born ~1890) matches GRO birth Q2 1890. FindAGrave headstone says aged 65 at death Dec 1954, implying birth ~1889 — off by ~6 months (born Apr-Jun 1890, died Dec 1954 = age 64, headstone says 65).
individuals.source_citations: 1901 Census (Source 213, ark:/61903/1:1:X9H6-B2B); FindAGrave memorial #192271800 (Source 215); 1891 Census Lambeth as Henry Parsons (Source 237, ark:/61903/1:1:QC6P-BT2); GRO Birth Registration Index Q2 1890 Lambeth (Source 238, ark:/61903/1:1:2XY1-XNG)
individuals.interesting_facts: His residence in Sturminster Newton, Dorset likely drew his brother William's family to evacuate from London to the same town during WWII, where Beryl Richards (Pauline's mother) was born in 1944.

individuals.residence: 1881: St George The Martyr Southwark; 1891: St George The Martyr Southwark (St Saviour Southwark registration district)
individuals.notes: Father of William Richards (ID 183, born ~1866 Lambeth). Identified through 1881 census at St George The Martyr Southwark. Age 42 in 1881 (born ~1839). Birthplace recorded as Southwark, Surrey in census; FamilySearch tree (LXQS-P1Y) gives birthplace as Lambeth, Surrey — the two districts are adjacent and the boundary was often blurred. Occupation: Tinman (a worker in tin/sheet metal). FamilySearch PID: LXQS-P1Y. 1891 CENSUS (Source 229): Still living at St George The Martyr Southwark with wife Mary (age 50), and children William (22), Eliza (24), Emma (19), James (14), Betsy (12). Eldest daughter Mary (age ~26) no longer in household. Occupation recorded as 'Iron & Tin Plate Worker' — same trade as 'Tinman' from 1881. Age 52 in 1891 (born ~1839), consistent with 1881 age 42 (born ~1839). Registration district: St Saviour Southwark. Piece/Folio: 344/161, RG12. Household ID 655715. PARENTS IDENTIFIED via 1851 Census (FindMyPast GBC/1851/0004652309): father James Richards (tinman, b. ~1813, Cripplegate, Middlesex) and mother Elizabeth Richards (b. ~1815, Rotherhithe, Surrey). In 1851, William (age 12) was a scholar living with parents and 7 siblings in Christchurch, St Saviour Southwark. 1871 CENSUS (FindMyPast GBC/1871/0003870524): William Richards, Head, age 30, Tin plate worker, born Christ Church Surrey, living in St Mary Newington with wife Mary (28, b. St George Martyr) and children Mary (7), William (4), Lizer A (3).
individuals.source_citations: 1881 Census (Source 224, ark:/61903/1:1:Q27Q-QPVS); FamilySearch tree LXQS-P1Y; 1891 Census (Source 229, ark:/61903/1:1:QHF8-GMM)
individuals.interesting_facts: Three generations of William Richards: this William (tinman, b. ~1839), his son William (carman, b. ~1866, ID 183), and his grandson William Joshua M (b. 1902, ID 60).

Father of William Richards (ID 191, tinman). Identified via 1851 Census (FindMyPast GBC/1851/0004652309). Occupation: Tinman. Born ~1813 in Cripplegate, Middlesex (City of London). In 1851, lived in Christchurch parish, St Saviour Southwark, with wife Elizabeth and 8 children: James (17, tinman), William (12, our ancestor), Maria (11), Christian (8), George (7), Thomas (6), Edward (5), Alice (infant). The tinsmith trade passed from father to son — both James Sr. and James Jr. listed as tinmen. 1841 census: living at Charlotte St, Great Broadwall, Christ Church, Southwark with wife Elizabeth and children James, William, Maria, Christian, and Elizabeth Pissell (b.~1799, possible mother-in-law). DEATH CONFIRMED: GRO Death Index Jun 1880, St Saviour Southwark, Vol 1d, Page 25, age 68 (born ~1812). Two possible Non-Conformist birth records in Cripplegate: (1) 31 Dec 1813, parents James Richards + Barbara Richards (ark:/61903/1:1:FWPS-GL2); (2) 29 Jun 1813, parents Robert Richards + Elizabeth Richards. Neither confirmed — need to order GRO death certificate to identify parents.
individuals.interesting_facts: Tinman by trade, same occupation as his son William (ID 191). Lived in Christchurch Southwark.

individuals.notes: Father of Lucy Harthorn (ID 418). Multiple conflicting sources about his parents: Geni lists parents as Silas Hathorn (1732-1787) + Hannah, but another source says David Hathorn + Abigail Burley. Wife's name also uncertain: some sources say Lucy Pitcher, others say Nancy Pitcher. Children include Lucy Spencer (b. Hathorn), David F, Abigail, Nancy, Rebecca, Reuben C, and Jane. Parents CONFIRMED as Silas Hathorn (1732-1787) and Hannah via Geni. Previous conflict resolved — Ashbel was son of Silas directly (not grandson through David). Silas was early Bangor settler who arrived 1772. Session 2026-04-08.

individuals.immigration: Emigrated from England. First appears in Martha's Vineyard records 1653.

individuals.occupation: Clerk of Courts, Justice of County Courts

individuals.residence: Watertown, then Rehoboth, then Martha's Vineyard

individuals.immigration: Emigrated to New England, established settlement on Martha's Vineyard 1642
individuals.notes: Governor Thomas Mayhew the Elder. Baptized 1 Apr 1593 Tisbury, Wiltshire, son of Mathew Mayhew and Alice Barter. Established the first European settlement on Martha's Vineyard, Nantucket, and adjacent islands in 1642. Purchased proprietorship of the islands. Married (1) unknown, (2) Jane Gallion/Galland. WikiTree Mayhew-74. Banks Vol. III pp. 298-328.
individuals.interesting_facts: Governed Martha's Vineyard for 40 years. His son Thomas Mayhew Jr was a missionary to the Wampanoag who was lost at sea in 1657.

individuals.interesting_facts: Daughter of Plymouth Colony Governor Thomas Hinckley

individuals.immigration: Arrived 1640 in Barnstable from Stretton, Rutlandshire, England

individuals.notes: Daughter of Rev. John Mayo (first pastor of Boston's Second Church) and Tamasin (Brike) Mayo. Married (1) Nathaniel Bacon Sr 4 Dec 1642 Barnstable; after his death ~1673 married (2) Gov. Thomas Prence of Plymouth Colony. WikiTree Mayo-39.
individuals.interesting_facts: Daughter of Rev. John Mayo; second husband was Governor Thomas Prence of Plymouth Colony

individuals.occupation: Governor of Plymouth Colony (1681-1692); Deputy; Assistant
individuals.immigration: Arrived New England ~1635 as teenager with father Samuel Hinckley
individuals.notes: Baptized 19 Mar 1618 at Hawkhurst, Kent, England (John Lothropp's church). Parents Samuel Hinckley and Sarah Soole were followers of the Nonconformist minister John Lothropp. Settled first Scituate then Barnstable. Married (1) Mary Richards 4 Dec 1641 Barnstable; she died 24 Jun 1659. Married (2) Mary (widow of Nathaniel Glover of Dorchester) 16 Mar 1660. Became Governor of Plymouth Colony upon Winslow's death 1680; served until Plymouth was merged into Massachusetts Bay in 1692. FS PID / Geni 6000000003484308787; WikiTree Hinckley-53. FindAGrave #6798664.
individuals.interesting_facts: Last Governor of Plymouth Colony before it was merged with Massachusetts Bay Colony in 1692.
research_leads: Extend Hinckley line - grandfather Robert Hinckley (~1537-1606) of Harrietsham
Extend Soole line - maternal grandparents Thomas Soole + Mary Iddenden of Hawkhurst

individuals.immigration: Arrived New England with father Thomas Richards ~1632 (Weymouth, MA)
individuals.notes: Baptized before 12 Nov 1620 Pitminster, Somerset. Daughter of Thomas Richards and Welthian (Loring) Richards of Pitminster/Weymouth. Married Thomas Hinckley 4 Dec 1641 Barnstable (some sources give 6 or 7 Dec). Mother of Sarah Hinckley (655) b. 1646 and at least seven other children. WikiTree Richards-468.
research_leads: Extend Richards line further - Thomas Richards Sr's father Thomas Richards the elder (~1571-1620) of Pitminster

individuals.occupation: Puritan minister; teaching elder Barnstable; pastor Eastham (1646-1655); first pastor Second (Old North) Church, Boston (1655-1673)
individuals.residence: Barnstable, Eastham, then Boston; died at son Samuel's home in Yarmouth
individuals.immigration: Arrived New England 1638
individuals.notes: Baptized 16 Oct 1597 as 'John Maio' (probably Farthinghoe, Northamptonshire). Educated at Magdalen College, Oxford. Married Tamasin Brike ~1618 (possibly Leiden, Holland — Dutch Reformed Church record disputed). Came to Boston 1638, Barnstable 1639 as teaching elder, Eastham 1646 as pastor, Second Church Boston 1655. Retired 1673. Died May 1676. Children: Hannah, Samuel, John, Nathaniel, Elizabeth. Wikipedia 'John Mayo (minister)'; WikiTree Mayo-13.
individuals.interesting_facts: First pastor of Boston's Second Church (Old North Church), predecessor of the Revolutionary-era church.

Mary Howland 1673), Mehitable (bapt 26 Nov 1648, m. Samuel Annable 1667 then Cornelius Briggs 1683). individuals.notes: Wife of Thomas Allyn of Barnstable. Maiden name unknown — some trees say Longman but this is unsourced. She had two daughters (Sarah and Rebecca) by a prior marriage who are mentioned as stepdaughters in Thomas Allyn's will. With Thomas she had Samuel (1643), John (1646), and Mehitable (1648). Died before Thomas's will was written 28 Feb 1674/5. Geni 6000000004237839369; WikiTree Unknown-13482.

individuals.immigration: Arrived Charlestown, Massachusetts 1634 on the Griffin with husband Thomas Lynde
individuals.notes: Second wife of Deacon Thomas Lynde of Charlestown. Previously married Henry Jordan, Citizen and Cutler of London, who died before 15 May 1632. Married Thomas Lynde 27 Nov 1632 at All Hallows, Bread Street, London. Burial record at Charlestown gives age 62 years 6 months, so born ~Feb 1599/1600. Maiden name disputed: older sources (and the London burial record) give 'Martin' or 'Martine'; Robert Charles Anderson argues for 'Hudson' based on additional London records. Mother (with Thomas Lynde) of six children: John, Joseph, Sarah (Pierpont), Hannah (Trerice/Kelling), Samuel, Henry. FindAGrave #36821512; WikiTree Hudson-5341.
individuals.interesting_facts: Widow of a London cutler who married Deacon Thomas Lynde and emigrated to Massachusetts on the Griffin.
research_leads: Resolve maiden name — Martin vs Hudson (Anderson Great Migration argues Hudson)

individuals.immigration: Sailed from Weymouth, England to New England on ship Hopewell, 8 May 1635
individuals.notes: Baptized 15 Apr 1596 Pitminster, Somerset, son of Thomas Richards the elder (~1571-~1620). First six children baptized at Pitminster 1620-1632. Sailed on Hopewell from Weymouth, England to New England 8 May 1635. Settled Weymouth, MA by ~1639; owned a mill in common with Henry Waltham. Selectman of Weymouth 1643. Will dated 17 Dec 1650; inventory taken 25 Jan 1650/1 valued at £1300 17s 11d including £200 real estate. Daughter Mary Richards became first wife of Gov. Thomas Hinckley. WikiTree Richards-189; Geni 6000000000682016965; FindAGrave #35354306.
research_leads: Identify Thomas Richards Sr's mother (wife of Thomas Richards the elder 678) — unidentified; 'Alice Hulins' appears in secondary sources but likely conflation
Verify Thomas the elder's parents James Richards + Alice Blake — currently unsourced

individuals.immigration: Arrived New England with husband Thomas Richards 1635
individuals.notes: Wife of Thomas Richards Sr (665) of Pitminster, Somerset and later Weymouth, MA. Mentioned as 'wife Wealthian' in Thomas's 1650 will. Mother of Mary Richards (659) and at least six other children baptized at Pitminster between 1620 and 1632. WikiTree Loring (as wife of Richards-189).
research_leads: DEAD-END (2026-04-20): Welthian's parentage unverified. Thomas Loring + Jane Newton of Axminster hypothesis is chronologically problematic (Thomas b ~1600 vs Welthian b ~1599-1602). Thomas Richards' will reference to Thomas Loring as 'brother' may mean brother-in-law (sister relationship) not father. See Source 417.
If pursuing: search Axminster and Pitminster parish registers for any Loring baptisms ~1599-1602 that match Welthian's profile

individuals.immigration: Arrived Plymouth Colony 1635 on ship Hercules, from Tenterden, Kent

A follower of Nonconformist minister John Lothropp; emigrated on Hercules 1635 from Tenterden, Kent; settled Scituate MA, then moved to Barnstable with Lothropp's congregation 1639. Died 'the end of October 1662' Barnstable. Father of Gov. Thomas Hinckley (658). WikiTree Hinckley-25; Geni 6000000002545933782; FindAGrave #260746194.

individuals.immigration: Arrived Plymouth Colony 1635 on Hercules with husband Samuel

individuals.notes: William Bacon of Stretton, Rutland, England. Named in a New Haven Colony court deposition of John Fletcher and others of Milford, taken 17 Oct 1661, which stated that William had only one brother, Henry Bacon, that they both lived in Stretton, Rutland; and that Nathaniel Bacon then present at New Haven was William's oldest son. Colonial Records of New Haven p 297. Wife Ann (maiden unknown). Never emigrated; died in England. WikiTree Bacon-123.

individuals.notes: Founder of the Wadhurst branch of the Whitfield family. Buried Wadhurst 2 Jun 1541. First marriage: Katherine Wenbourne (widow), three daughters. Second marriage: Agnes Giles, three sons (Robert ~1517, John ~1520, Thomas ~1524) and a daughter (Elizabeth 1522). His son John Whitfield I (708) is the second son. Established as ironmaster after migrating from Cumberland.
individuals.source_citations: Source 434: WikiTree Whitfield-84; Whitfield One-Name Study Section 2; Wikipedia 'Whitfield family'; Geni 6000000006437514894; FindAGrave 136796733.
individuals.interesting_facts: Reportedly aged 88 at death — exceptional longevity for the era. Migrated from Cumberland to Sussex and became a successful ironmaster, establishing the Wadhurst Whitfields as iron magnates of the Sussex Weald. The Whitfield ironmaster tradition continued through his son Robert (the Younger) at Worth, Sussex and Rowfant House.

The Whitfield One-Name Study positions 725 as 'brother or cousin of Sir Matthew gen 11' (i.e., same generation, not parent-child). The Bryan-Smith / WikiTree-58 reading appears to conflate two different William Whitfields. No parents added this session pending primary-source resolution.

individuals.occupation: knight/landowner; held the Randalholme manor house, Aldstonmoor lands, and Kirkhaugh parish lands as tenant via his wife (a Vipont/Vetripont granddaughter)
individuals.notes: Father of Maud Whitley (726). Per Whitfield One-Name Study Randalholme Generations 12-15, William 'died leaving three daughters' -- Maud was one (she received Randalholme as her portion). His wife was a granddaughter of Robert de Vipont/Vetripont (d. 13 Jul 1371); the Vipont line brought the Cumberland Alston Moor / Kirkhaugh lands into the Whitley family. The 1570s witness testimony refers to him as 'John Whetlay or Whitley' rather than William; the One-Name Study uses 'William de Whitelaw' from compiled sources. Death inferred to be before c.1410 since by that date Maud is married to William Whitfield (725) and the Randalholme inheritance had already passed to her. Wife identified 2026-04-28: Joan (Vipont) de Whitlawe (737), b. before 1346, daughter of Nicholas de Vipont (738) and Elena (Aubeney) de Vipont (739), and granddaughter of Robert de Vipont (1303-1371). Per WikiTree Vipont-28. The Randalholme inheritance descended through Joan, not William.
individuals.interesting_facts: Through his (unnamed) wife, William transmitted the Randalholme estate -- originally Raynerholme -- to the Whitfield line, which in turn inherited Alston Moor lands tracing back to Robert de Vipont (d. 1371) and the medieval Vipont/Vetripont barony.

Identify William's parents -- the 'de Whitelaw' surname suggests a Northumberland/Cumberland origin; check Bain's Calendar of Documents Relating to Scotland and Northumberland Pipe Rolls for de Whitelaw entries c.1340-1380. 2026-04-28 web search did not surface them.
Identify the other two Whitley daughters (Maud's sisters) -- they would have inherited the other Vipont/Whitley shares.
Trace Vipont line one further generation back: Robert de Vipont (1303-1371, WikiTree Vipont-25) is Joan (737)'s grandfather; the deep Vipont line continues from there.

individuals.notes: Father of John 'The Elder' Hinckley/Hinclyne/Henclive of Henhann (730), per Geni profile https://www.geni.com/people/John-Hinckley-Hinclyne-Henclive/6000000003215278431 . Lived at Lenham, Kent. Connects the Lenham John (~1300) to The Elder (~1375).
individuals.interesting_facts: Bridge generation in the Hinckley/Hinclyne pedigree at Lenham, Kent.
research_leads: Locate Lenham parish manorial / archdeaconry court records c.1380-1410 for John Hinckley/Hinclyne/Henclive of Lenham.
Identify wife (mother of John 'The Elder') -- not surfaced on Geni snippet.

individuals.occupation: Member, Cinque Ports (Tenham, Kent); landholder at Mellefeld (Tenham) and Gore & Pete
individuals.notes: Earliest documented Hinckley ancestor. Per Geni profile https://www.geni.com/people/John-Hinckley-Hinclyne-of-Lenham/6000000042520391316 . Documented as 'A John de Henclyne of Tenham, Kent, member of the Cinque Ports, assessed for Knighting the Black Prince in 1346, paid 20/- in 1348 for the farm of Gore & Pete and 16/- for the rent of Mellefeld (Tenham) to the steward of the Archbishop of Canterbury.' This Mellefeld property reappears in John 'The Elder's' 1463 will, anchoring the descent across three generations.
individuals.interesting_facts: Member of the Cinque Ports federation (Tenham was within the influence of the Cinque Ports). The 1346 Black Prince Knighting Aid -- a feudal tax assessed when the future Edward, the Black Prince was knighted -- placed him in the documentary record. Mellefeld stayed in Hinckley hands for at least 117 years (1348-1463/64).
research_leads: Locate the original 1346 Knighting Aid roll for Tenham hundred (TNA E 179/?) and the 1348 Archbishop of Canterbury steward's account roll for Gore & Pete + Mellefeld -- both are primary sources naming John de Henclyne.
Identify parents -- pre-1300 Henclyne records may exist in Canterbury Cathedral / Lambeth archives but are unindexed online.
Confirm whether 'John de Henclyne' of 1346 is identical to the c.1300-born John or his father; possible single-generation collapse in compiled trees.

individuals.notes: Wife of John Howe I (732) of Hawkhurst, Kent. Mother of John Howe II (723). Per Geni profile https://www.geni.com/people/Lady-Bette-Howe/6000000055012025046 -- single compiled-tree source. The 'Lady' honorific is unverified and is recorded only as an alias here. Maiden surname 'De Huff' is uncommon and may be a Geni artifact pending primary-source corroboration. Held back from prior session (2026-04-27) due to single-source weakness; added 2026-04-28 after explicit Geni profile match.
research_leads: Verify maiden surname 'De Huff' -- search Hawkhurst / Cranbrook / Tenterden parish-adjacent records for any De Huff / Hough / Huff family active c.1380-1410 in Kent.
Verify the 'Lady' honorific via primary documentation -- if real, would imply knightly or noble parentage worth tracing.

individuals.notes: Wife of William de Whitelaw / Whitley (733). Mother of Maud Whitley (726) and at least two other Whitley daughters (Maud's portion was Randalholme). Per WikiTree Vipont-28 https://www.wikitree.com/wiki/Vipont-28 : Joan was a granddaughter of Robert de Vipont (1303-1371, Vipont-25), heiress (with her sister Elizabeth, who married Thomas de Blencowe) of the Vipont/Vetripont Cumberland estates after her grandfather's 1371 death. Her marriage carried the Randalholme share into the Whitelaw/Whitley line; the next generation (Maud) carried it into the Whitfield line via her marriage to William Whitfield (725). She also had a brother Robert who died 1371 holding the manor of Johnby.
individuals.interesting_facts: Through Joan, the Vipont/Vetripont medieval barony of Westmorland is linked into the Whitley/Whitfield/Frost line. The Vipont line traces back to Ivo de Vieuxpont (Norman, 11th c.) and includes hereditary sheriffs of Westmorland.
research_leads: Confirm Joan's exact birth and death dates -- WikiTree gives only 'before 1346'.
Identify Joan's specific share of the 1371 Vipont inheritance vs. her sister Elizabeth's share (which went to the Blencowe line).
Trace Vipont line further back via WikiTree Vipont-25 (Robert, 1303-1371) -> Vipont-1 (John, c.1212-1241) -> Vipont-17 (Robert, c.1164-1228) -> Vipont-10 (William, c.1140-1200).

individuals.notes: Father of Joan (Vipont) de Whitlawe (737). Per WikiTree Vipont-28 (Joan's profile). Eldest son of Robert de Vipont (1303-1371, Vipont-25); predeceased his father by ~9 years. Married Elena (Aubeney) (739) per WikiTree Aubeney-3. Their two daughters (Joan and Elizabeth) became co-heiresses of the Vipont/Vetripont Cumberland estates after Robert's 1371 death -- the inheritance skipped Nicholas's death generation.
individuals.interesting_facts: Possibly the same Nicholas of Westmorland documented as eldest son of Robert de Vipont (1303-1371) per the Vipont-25 WikiTree summary; predeceased his father, creating the inheritance situation that distributed the Vipont estates among his daughters.
research_leads: Locate the 1362 inquisition post mortem (IPM) for Nicholas de Vipont in TNA C 134/? records.
Identify Nicholas's father Robert de Vipont (1303-1371, WikiTree Vipont-25) as a future tree extension if Kevin wants to push the Vipont line further.

individuals.notes: Mother of Joan (Vipont) de Whitlawe (737) and Elizabeth (Vipont) de Blencowe. Wife of Nicholas de Vipont (738). Per WikiTree Aubeney-3 https://www.wikitree.com/wiki/Aubeney-3 . Aubeney/d'Aubigny was a notable Anglo-Norman family -- her exact parentage within that line has not been pinned to primary sources via this session.
individuals.interesting_facts: Aubeney/d'Aubigny lineage may connect this branch to the medieval Earls of Arundel.
research_leads: Identify Elena's specific Aubeney parentage -- WikiTree Aubeney-3 should be checked directly via Chrome (not currently accessible from this sandbox).
Verify the c.1327-1362 dates from primary records (likely an IPM after death).

sources.description: Find a Grave — William Biddle Frank, memorial #118358101, Bridgeport Cemetery, Bridgeport, Gloucester County, NJ. Birth 1869, death 14 Feb 1920. Headstone photo: 'WILLIAM B. FRANK 1869 — 1920'. Parents: Jacob Franks. Spouse: Mary Elizabeth Farnkopf Frank (m. 1895). 6 children listed (Joseph and Hannah/Anna not included on memorial). Photo added by Guy; memorial added by Gary Chaffin.
sources.location: Bridgeport Cemetery, Bridgeport, Gloucester County, NJ
sources.notes: Children on memorial: William T (1896-1974), Augustus Farenkoph (1899-1986), Harry Henry (1902-1992), George Reed Sr (1903-1988), Warren DuBois (1908-1966), Albert Jacob (1912-2001). Joseph Franklin and Anna/Hannah not listed.

sources.description: Find a Grave — Mary Elizabeth Farnkopf Frank, memorial #118358084, Bridgeport Cemetery, Bridgeport, Gloucester County, NJ. Birth 6 Nov 1877, Salem, Salem County, NJ. Death 7 Mar 1956 (aged 78), Philadelphia, Philadelphia County, PA. Headstone photo: 'MARY E. FRANK 1877 — 1956'. Obituary clipping attached: 'Mrs. Mary E. Frank Dies in Philadelphia' — died Wednesday in St. Joseph's Hospital, Philadelphia; resident of 85 Main Street, Bridgeport for 60 years. Survived by daughter Mrs. Hannah E. Craft of Northport, NY, and seven sons: William T., August, Harry and Albert of Bridgeport; Joseph of Swedesboro; George of Repauno; Warren of Pedricktown. Services at Carlton Coles Funeral Home, Swedesboro. Interment at Bridgeport Cemetery. Memorial added by Gary Chaffin; obituary clipping added by Tom Jennings.
sources.location: Bridgeport Cemetery, Bridgeport, Gloucester County, NJ
sources.notes: Obituary published ~March 9, 1956 (Bridgeport, N.J., March 9 — Special). Confirms 8 surviving children in 1956. Mother listed as Mary Ellen Haverly Farenkopf (1848-1920). Parents confirm Augustus Farenkopf (1847-1916) and Mary Ellen Haverly.

sources.description: US Census 1920 — William B Frank household, Gloucester, NJ. Head: William B Frank, age 50, married, born NJ, father born Germany, mother born Germany. Wife: Mary L Frank, age 42, NJ. Children at home: William T (23), Harry D (17), George R (16), Anna S (14), Warren D (11), Albert J (8), Joseph F (4). (FamilySearch ark:/61903/1:1:M458-JB4)
sources.notes: Augustus (age ~21) not in household — likely moved out. Laura (d. 1898 infant) deceased. Anna S Frank (age 14) is likely Hannah E. Craft from Mary's obituary.

sources.description: England and Wales Census 1891 — William Richards, Head, age 52, Iron & Tin Plate Worker, born London. Wife: Mary, age 50, born London. Children: Eliza (24), William (22), Emma (19), James (14), Betsy (12). All born London. St George The Martyr Southwark, Southwark, London. Registration District: St Saviour Southwark. Piece/Folio: 344/161, RG12. Household ID 655715.
sources.location: St George The Martyr Southwark, London, England
sources.notes: Confirms tinman William Richards (ID 191) and family still in same Southwark location as 1881. Son William (ID 183) age 22 still at home with no wife — he was not yet married to Sarah. FamilySearch tree match: LXQS-P1Y.

sources.description: England and Wales Marriage Registration Index — William Richards married Sarah Parsons, Q2 1893 (Apr-May-Jun), Lambeth, London. Vol 1D, p 709.
sources.notes: Cross-referenced: both William Richards and Sarah Parsons entries share identical Vol 1D, p 709, Q2 1893, Lambeth — confirming they married each other. Possible co-registrants also listed (Alice Davies, William Sturgeon) but the Richards-Parsons pairing is confirmed by the matching page numbers. Reveals Sarah's maiden name as PARSONS. From 'England & Wales Marriages, 1837-2005' via findmypast, sourced from General Register Office, Southport.

sources.description: England and Wales Death Registration Index — William Richards, age 59, 1899, St. Saviour Southwark, London. Vol 1D, p 64.
sources.notes: TENTATIVE match for William Richards the tinman (ID 191). Age 59 gives birth ~1840, close to ~1839 from census records. Same registration district (St Saviour Southwark) as 1891 census. However, William Richards is a common name — needs verification via GRO death certificate order. Not yet linked to tree individual.

sources.description: United States Census 1860 — Anna Everetts, age 3, in household of Alanthus W Everetts (31, NY) and Harriet M Everetts (28, NY), Dix, Schuyler County, NY. Also in household: Mary Everetts (6), James Everetts (4), Polly L Crawford (29), Harriet M Crawford (3). Page 39, NARA M653.
sources.notes: Surname indexed as 'Everetts' — exact match to maiden name in tree. Attached to FamilySearch tree person GN1Q-71S (Anna M. Everts).

sources.description: United States Census 1880 — Anna M. Everts, age 23, single, occupation 'At Home', daughter, in household of Aranthus W. Everts (51, NY) and Harriet Everts (47, NY), Montour, ED 149, Schuyler County, NY. Siblings in household: James L (24), Hannah (19), Alpha E (14), Eva C (14), Aranthus W (11), Clara H (7). Father's birthplace: NY. Mother's birthplace: NY. Sheet A, #388.
sources.notes: CRITICAL: Anna M. Everts listed as SINGLE in 1880, still living with parents. She married Franklin Miller ~1881 after this census. Attached to FamilySearch tree person GN1Q-71S.

sources.description: United States Census 1860 — John F Miller, age 5, born ~1855, New York, in household of Mary Miller (45, Connecticut) as head, William Miller (36, NY), Emmeline Miller (19, NY), Harrison Miller (17, NY), Mary L Miller (8, NY). Catharine, Schuyler County, NY. Page 43, NARA M653.
sources.notes: John F Miller (age 5, born ~1855) is likely our Franklin J Miller (ID 171). 'John F' could be John Franklin or Franklin with middle initial J. Catharine township is adjacent to Hector in Schuyler County. No 'Robert' or 'Lucy' in this household — conflicts with NUMIDENT parent names.

sources.description: England and Wales Birth Registration Index — Sarah J Parsons, Q4 1866 (Oct-Nov-Dec), St. Saviour Southwark, London. Vol 1D, Page 13. Citing General Register Office, Southport, England. Via findmypast.
sources.notes: Birth registration for Sarah J Parsons (later Sarah Richards, ID 184). St. Saviour Southwark is the same registration district as the Richards tinman family.

sources.description: England and Wales Census 1891 — Sarah Parsons, Head, Female, age 25, Widowed, Washer, born Surrey. Son: Henry Parsons, Male, age 1, born Surrey. Lambeth, London. Enumeration District 28, Piece/Folio 401/109, RG12. Household 760295.
sources.notes: Sarah Parsons (future Sarah Richards, ID 184) living as head of household in Lambeth with infant son Henry Parsons (future Henry Richards, ID 185). Listed as 'widowed' — likely a social convention; no prior marriage record found. FamilySearch tree PID 9WF8-QGF.

sources.description: England and Wales Birth Registration Index — Henry Hector Parsons, Q2 1890 (Apr-May-Jun), Lambeth, London. Vol 1D, Page 390. Citing General Register Office, Southport, England. Via findmypast.

sources.description: WikiTree Fuller-117 / Geni / FindAGrave #34104400 — Capt. Matthew Fuller (~1605-1678) of Barnstable, Plymouth Colony. Married Frances (possibly Iyde) ~1625 in England. Will 25 Jul 1678 names wife Frances and daughter Elizabeth Rowley.
sources.notes: Also: https://www.findagrave.com/memorial/34104400/matthew-fuller. Captain of Plymouth forces in King Philip's War 1675. First physician at Barnstable. Estate appraised at £1,667.

sources.description: Fuller Big Y DNA Project findings, published Summer 2022 Mayflower Descendant. Conclusively proved Matthew Fuller of Barnstable was NOT a son of Edward Fuller of the Mayflower. Lineage applications via Matthew closed by General Society of Mayflower Descendants.
sources.notes: Summer 2024 Mayflower Descendant proposed new origin: Matthew baptized 24 Feb 1613 Redenhall, son of John Fuller (nephew of Robert Fuller, father of Edward). Still under research.

sources.description: WikiTree Weeks-291 / Geni — William Weeks (~1620-~1689) of Edgartown, Martha's Vineyard. First appears in 1653 land division. Granted land 1655. Died between Dec 1688 and Aug 1689. Operated a tavern.
sources.notes: Also: https://www.geni.com/people/William-Weeks-of-Edgartown/5357456914240112309. First wife name unknown (died before 1658). Second wife Mary Lynde (widow of John Butler). No will or probate found.
