sources.description: Gavin Spencer Whitney obituary, Oct 2014. Guare & Sons Funeral Home, Montpelier, VT. Names family: parents Spencer and Patricia Whitney; predeceased by uncle Douglas Nicholson, grandparents Betty and Clifton Whitney, Mae Nicholson; survived by mother Patricia, aunts and uncles Jean and Steve Purdy, Gail Frank, Rev. Ann Whitney.
sources.notes: Key source for identifying all four Whitney children and their spouses/titles.

sources.description: Patricia Ann Whitney obituary, Apr 2024. Guare & Sons Funeral Home, Montpelier, VT. Born Aug 13, 1946 Rutland, VT to Harold Glenn and Laura Mae Nicholson. Married Spencer Hale Whitney Mar 28, 1967. Survived by daughter Lynnette (Paul) Claudon of Monkton.
sources.notes: Confirms Spencer's marriage date, Patricia's parents, and daughter Lynnette.

sources.description: Kevin Frank, 2026-04-15. Provided: (1) Clifton and Mary Whitney had 4 children: Spencer, Gail, Jean, Ann. Spencer died, others alive. Gail was superintendent of correctional institutions in Alaska. (2) Mildred Frank (134) married first into Arndt family, had 4 sons: Edward, Raymond, Stephen, and one who died young ~14-15 in 1980s. Second marriage unknown. Mildred died recently.

Joseph D. Frank (136) family research. Kevin provided: married Sue Frank (father was NJ Supreme Court Justice); 4 children Kimberly, Andrew, Amy, Jonathan. Web research confirmed: (1) Jonathan Frank is EVP FX Current Series & FX Productions at FX Networks, Brown University BA Psychology 1994-1998, married Meredith Milton, children Wyatt and Greer, resides Pacific Palisades CA. (2) Andrew Scott Frank is Senior Partner at FrankLeonards&Frank LLC, Woodstown NJ, financial advisor with Cambridge Investment Research, attended Western State College in Gunnison CO, returned to NJ 2004. (3) Joseph D. Frank confirmed as Senior Partner at same firm, SEC CRD #212791. Sue Frank's maiden name and father (NJ Supreme Court Justice) not identified despite searching justices serving 1960s-1990s. Amy Frank not found in Philadelphia utility attorney searches — may practice under married name. Kimberly Frank possibly attended Rider University (Communications) — unconfirmed.
sources.notes: Sources: Big Screen Symposium bio (Jonathan Frank); Produced By Conference bio; Deadline.com 2016-12; FrankLeonards&Frank LLC website; SEC IAPD reports; Kevin Frank family knowledge

sources.description: Ensign Thomas Lynde — WikiTree Lynde-6; FindAGrave #31170139; kristinhall.org Lynde page; Geneanet gsprague; connectedbloodlines.com
sources.notes: Multiple sources confirm Ensign Thomas Lynde b. 7 Feb 1615 Dunstable, d. 15 Oct 1693 Malden. Wife Elizabeth (possibly Tufts), married ~1647 Charlestown.

sources.description: Anna Baker parentage — WikiTree Baker-1458 (disconnected Richard Baker + Margaret Elkes); Baker-3314 Alexander Baker; Geni; genealogieonline.nl Family Tree Keith; emptybranchesonthefamilytree.com Bayes article
sources.notes: Anna Baker's parents identified as Alexander Baker and Frances (Grigg) Pendleton. The Richard Baker + Margaret Elkes claim has been disconnected by WikiTree as unsupported. Anna married Thomas Bayes 26 Dec 1639 Dedham.

sources.description: Howes family — WikiTree Howes-43, Howes-67; Miner Descent Thomas Howes and Jeremiah Howes pages; Genealogy of the Howes Family (1892); FindAGrave #8515586; howesfamilies.com
sources.notes: Thomas Howes (~1601-1665) and wife Mary (Burr?) arrived ~1637 with 3 sons. Jeremiah born at sea ~1637. Jeremiah married (1) Sarah Prence ~1656, (2) Mary Daggett ~1693. Date conflict: Sarah Prence reportedly d. 1707 but Mary Daggett marriage given as ~1693.

sources.description: Annable family — WikiTree Annable-83, Annable-33, Annable-3; FindAGrave #52433295; Geni
sources.notes: Samuel Annable (1669-1744) son of Samuel (1646-1678) and Mehitable Allyn. Anthony Annable arrived Plymouth Colony 1623, married (2) Ann Clark 1644/5.

sources.description: Bacon family — WikiTree Bacon-95, Hinckley-137; Geni; genealogieonline.nl Genealogy Ludwig; FindAGrave #55845342; Genealogical Notes of Barnstable Families
sources.notes: Nathaniel Bacon Sr (~1621-1673) from Stretton, Rutland; m. Hannah Mayo 4 Dec 1642 Barnstable. Son Nathaniel II (1645-1691) m. Sarah Hinckley (daughter of Gov. Thomas Hinckley) 27 Mar 1673. Grandson Nathaniel III (1674-1738) m. Ruth Daggett.

sources.description: Thomas Hinckley (Governor of Plymouth Colony) and wife Mary Richards — WikiTree Hinckley-53 (Thomas); Wikipedia 'Thomas Hinckley'; Geni ID 6000000003484308787; WikiTree Richards-468 (Marie). Establishes baptism 19 Mar 1618 Hawkhurst Kent; parents Samuel Hinckley + Sarah Soole; marriage 4 Dec 1641 Barnstable to Mary Richards (d/o Thomas Richards + Welthian Loring of Pitminster, Somerset).
sources.notes: Governor Thomas Hinckley was the last governor of Plymouth Colony before the 1692 merger with Massachusetts Bay.

sources.description: Rev. John Mayo and wife Tamasin Brike — Wikipedia 'John Mayo (minister)'; WikiTree Mayo-13; massandmoregenealogy.blogspot.com (Aug 2011); Geni 6000000003938789768. Establishes baptism 16 Oct 1597; Magdalen College Oxford; marriage to Tamasin 1618 Leiden; children Hannah, Samuel, John, Nathaniel, Elizabeth; minister Boston/Barnstable/Eastham/Boston-2nd-Church; died 1676.
sources.notes: Some debate about whether the Leiden 1618 marriage record is Rev. John Mayo's.

sources.description: Thomas Allyn/Allen of Braunton & Barnstable — WikiTree Allen-4709; Geni 6000000000484763851; MyHeritage; D. Joyce genealogy at Clark University (rr04_042). Baptized 24 Dec 1597 Braunton, Devon; died Barnstable between 28 Feb 1675 and 5 Mar 1680; wife Winifred (maiden unknown); children Samuel (bapt 18 Feb 1643), John (bapt 27 Sep 1646), Mehitable (bapt 26 Nov 1648).
sources.notes: Winifred also had children Sarah and Rebecca from a prior marriage (not Thomas Allyn's).

sources.description: Thomas Howes of Yarmouth — origin Norfolk, England (Morningthorpe tradition). Sources: Miner Descent (2010); familypedia Thomas_Howes_(1601-1665); Schow-Bickmore blog; massandmoregenealogy blog (2011). Confirms Norfolk (not Devonshire) as English origin but notes the JC Howes claim linking him to the Morningthorpe Howes family is disputed; parents remain unproven.
sources.location: Multiple genealogy blogs and compiled trees
sources.notes: Howes monument in Dennis, MA reportedly states births/deaths/marriages of his English ancestors are unknown.

sources.description: Margaret Jordan Lynde (2nd wife of Deacon Thomas Lynde) — FindAGrave Memorial #36821512; WikiTree Hudson-5341 (Anderson argues maiden Hudson); older sources give maiden as Martin. Born ~Feb 1599/1600, died 3 Aug 1662 Charlestown, age 62.5. Previously married Henry Jordan, Citizen and Cutler of London (d. before 15 May 1632). Married Thomas Lynde 27 Nov 1632 All Hallows Bread Street, London. 6 children with Lynde: John, Joseph, Sarah (Pierpont), Hannah (Trerice/Kelling), Samuel, Henry.
sources.notes: Maiden name disputed between Martin and Hudson; Robert Charles Anderson favors Hudson.

sources.description: WikiTree profile Richards-189 for Thomas Richards of Pitminster & Weymouth, MA; Geni, FindAGrave #35354306, FamilyCentral, Stagge-Parker Histories blog, Caskey-Family site
sources.notes: Covers baptism 15 Apr 1596 Pitminster, 1635 Hopewell voyage, 1650 will and 1650/1 probate; daughter Mary to Gov. Thomas Hinckley

sources.description: WikiTree Hinckley-25, Geni 6000000002545933782, FindAGrave #260746194, WikiTree Soole-10, Iddenden-3 for the Hinckley-Soole family of Harrietsham and Hawkhurst, Kent
sources.location: Harrietsham & Hawkhurst, Kent, England; Scituate & Barnstable, MA
sources.notes: Samuel Hinckley bap 25 May 1589 Harrietsham; Sarah Soole bap 8 Jun 1600 Hawkhurst; m. 7 May 1617 Hawkhurst; Hercules 1635 from Tenterden

sources.description: WikiTree Mayo-14 (John Mayo 1565-abt.1635), Geni 6000000006444392407, Thorpe Mandeville parish historical page, familypedia John_Mayo_(1598-1676)
sources.notes: Father buried Thorpe Mandeville 1629 (called carpenter in burial register); mother buried 1633; children baptized at Farthinghoe 1595-1601

sources.description: Colonial Records of New Haven p 297: affidavit of John Fletcher et al of Milford, 17 Oct 1661, naming Nathaniel Bacon as oldest son of William Bacon of Stretton, Rutland; cited through WikiTree Bacon-95/Bacon-123
sources.notes: Primary source naming William + Henry Bacon as brothers of Stretton, Rutland, and Nathaniel as William's oldest son

sources.description: WikiTree Momford-6, Geni 6000000006451531990, FindAGrave #88714898, hillfamilymd.org, dearbornboutwell rootsweb - Jane (Momford) Annable
sources.notes: First wife of Anthony Annable; m. 26 Apr 1619 All Saints, Cambridge; d. 13 Dec 1643 Barnstable; parents Thomas Momford + Joan Richardson per Geni (weak sourcing)

sources.description: Robert Hinckley (~1537-1606) of Harrietsham, Kent — parents of Samuel Hinckley (667). Second marriage to Katherine Leese (widow, possibly of Thomas Leese of Throwley) 10 Feb 1574/5 Harrietsham. Samuel bap 25 May 1587(/89) Harrietsham.
sources.notes: Robert b ~1537 Harrietsham; d 27 Mar 1606 Harrietsham. First wife Elizabeth buried 2 Nov 1574 Harrietsham. Katherine Leese (1551-1605) d before Robert. Robert's parents per WikiTree/Geni: John Hinckley (c.1512-c.1577) of Harrietsham + Johane Bills (1515-1563) — research lead only.

sources.description: Thomas Soole (~1567-1614) + Mary Iddenden (~1573-1656) of Hawkhurst, Kent — parents of Sarah Soole (668). Marriage 16 Oct 1598 St Laurence, Hawkhurst. Children Sarah 17 Jun 1600, Mary 29 Jan 1603, Thomas 12 May 1605, Anne 30 Apr 1615.
sources.notes: Thomas Soole's parents per WikiTree Soole-3: John Soole + Maria Whitfield. Mary Iddenden's parents per WikiTree Iddenden-3: John Iddenden + Christian Mercer. Both research leads.

sources.description: Thomas Richards the elder (~1571-~1620) of Pitminster, Somerset — father of Thomas Richards Sr (665). Per WikiTree Richards-1218. Parents hypothesized as James Richards (~1548-1608) + Alice Blake (~1548-1585) per WikiTree Richards-1128 and Blake-255.
sources.notes: James Richards married Alice Blake 2 Oct 1569 Over Stowey, Somerset; James buried 19 Jan 1607/8 Pitminster; Alice buried 17 Aug 1585 Pitminster. Thomas the elder's baptism not located in Pitminster register; connection to James+Alice is unsourced per WikiTree Richards-1218. Recorded as research lead only.

sources.description: Richard Allyn (~1563/4-1651) + Margaret Wyatt (1569-1642) of Braunton, Devon — parents of Thomas Allyn (662). Marriage 24 Sep 1583 Braunton. Children baptized Braunton: Thomas 2 Dec 1597 (also noted 23/24 Dec), Richard 6 May 1601, Matthew 17 Apr 1605. Richard buried 5 Aug 1651 Braunton. Margaret d. 1642 Braunton.
sources.notes: Richard Allyn's parents per some trees: Matthew Allen + Katherine. Margaret Wyatt's parents per WikiTree Wyatt-164: Walter Wyatt + Joan Pilford. Both research leads; primary-source verification needed.

sources.description: Welthian (Loring) Richards (666) — parentage remains unverified. WikiTree Loring-28 states parents unknown; lack of primary source. The hypothesis that she is daughter of Thomas Loring of Axminster (~1600-1661) is chronologically problematic (Thomas Loring born ~1600, Welthian born ~1599-1602). The 'brother' reference in Thomas Richards' will may indicate brother-in-law, making Welthian a sister (not daughter) of Thomas Loring.
sources.notes: Documented as a new dead-end in dead-end.md 2026-04-20 session.

sources.description: Parents of Robert Hinckley (674): John Hinckley (c.1512-1577) + 1st wife Johane Bills (c.1515-1563); m. 17 Nov 1553 Harrietsham, Kent. John's parents per sources: John (2) Henkle + Margaret Launcelyn (research lead).
sources.notes: Cross-referenced WikiTree Hynckleye-1 (John Hinckley c.1510-1577); Geni 6000000001620201968; Familypedia 'John_Hinckley_(c1511-1577)'; FindAGrave #176451891. Robert Hinckley (674) is a child of John's first marriage to Johane Bills (who died 1563). John remarried Aves Elles 3 Jul 1570 Harrietsham.

sources.description: Parents of Thomas Soole (676): John Soole (c.1540-1569) + Maria Whitfield (b.1544); m. 21 Jun 1565 Hawkhurst, Kent.
sources.notes: Cross-referenced WikiTree Soole-8 (John Soole); Geni 6000000006210854098; WikiTree Soole-3 (Thomas Soole, child); FindAGrave #152295783 (Mary Iddenden Soole). John Soole d. 1569 Hawkhurst age ~29.

sources.description: Parents of Mary Iddenden (677): John Iddenden (1544-1588) + Christian Mercer (c.1548-1597); m. 26 Sep 1569 Hawkhurst, Kent. Christian's parents: Thomas Mercer (1524-1585) + Joan Glover of Hawkhurst (research lead).
sources.notes: Cross-referenced WikiTree Iddenden-5 (John); WikiTree Iddenden-3 (Mary); Geni 6000000006913120368 (John) and 6000000006913279039 (Christian); member.tripod.com/~rjsnyder sub1/fam00161.htm. Christian (widow Iddenden) d. 13 Aug 1597 Cranbrook, Kent (plague). WikiTree Mercer-2140 gives Christian's father as Thomas Mercer.

sources.description: Parents of Richard Allyn of Braunton (679): Matthew Allen (bur. 7 Feb 1564/5 Braunton) + Katherine (d. 1565 late). No marriage record located; Matthew's own parents are unknown.
sources.notes: Cross-referenced WikiTree Allen-959 (Richard Allen, cites Matthew as father); Bolton, C. K., 'Ancestry of Margaret Wyatt, wife of Matthew Allyn of Braunton in Devon, and later of Windsor in Connecticut' (1898); Braunton parish burial register 7 Feb 1564/5 for Matthew; Katherine's burial also in 1565 per Bolton. Matthew's own parents not established.

sources.description: Parents of Margaret Wyatt (680): Walter Wyatt (1532-1610) + Joan Pilford (c.1536-1587); m. 17 Aug 1556 Braunton, Devon.
sources.notes: Cross-referenced WikiTree Wyatt-160 (Walter); WikiTree Pilford-1 (Joan); Geni 6000000007264182847 (Joan); Bolton 1898 'Ancestry of Margaret Wyatt'; werelate.org Walter_Wyatt_and_Joan_Pilford_(2). Siblings of Margaret: Phillip bap 24 Apr 1560 Braunton; an earlier 'Margaret' bap 19 May 1557 (may be the elder sister who died young, or a conflation).

sources.description: Thomas Mercer (1524-1585) + Joan Glover as parents of Christian Mercer — WikiTree Mercer-2140, Geni 6000000000421526066, genealogieonline.nl I88904956, Ancestry records. Multi-source corroboration.
sources.notes: WikiTree profile self-notes lack of primary sources; Thomas's own parents (Thomas Henry Mercer + Mary Joan Hatch) remain a research lead. Marriage date (1553 in one source) conflicts with Christian's 1548 birth — marriage must be before 1548.

sources.description: Phillip Wyatt (~1500-~1591/92) + Jane Paty as parents of Walter Wyatt — WikiTree Wyatt-161, Geni, genealogieonline.nl P10605, nielsenhayden.com genealogy-tng I6920, werelate.org Person:Philip_Wyatt. Multi-source corroboration.
sources.notes: Phillip served as Steward & Town Clerk of Barnstaple. His father reported as John Wyatt in some sources (research lead).

sources.description: Alexander Pilford + Margaret Allyn as parents of Joan Pilford — WikiTree Pilford-1, Geni 6000000007264182847, deloriahurst.com 3682, werelate.org Person:Joan_Pilford_(2). Multi-source corroboration.
sources.notes: Joan had a sister Margaret (m. Spurstowe). Primary parish records not verified.

sources.description: Henry Soole (~1500-~1540) + Agnes (maiden unknown) as parents of John Soole — WikiTree Soole-8, Geni 6000000006210854098 (John), Geni 6000000002208015011 (Henry). Ancestry records.
sources.notes: WikiTree Soole-8 self-notes lack of primary sources. Chronology tight but plausible (Henry d. 1540 = year of John's birth). Possible connection to John Soule (WikiTree Soule-494, 1500-1544) remains a research lead — NOT the same as Henry.

sources.description: Iddenden parents search — John Iddenden (1544 Cranbrook) parents unidentified in WikiTree Iddenden-5, Geni 6000000006913120368, ancestry.com records, ourfolkgen.com. William Iddenden (1536-1578) of Cranbrook is contemporaneous but too close in age to be John's father (probable sibling). Thomas Iddenden (d. 1556, Hawkhurst benefactor) could plausibly be father by chronology but no direct evidence.
sources.notes: Research lead only; no data change made to Iddenden line.

sources.description: Thomas Henry Mercer (~1485 Hawkhurst) + Mary Joan Hatch (~1498 Hawkhurst - 1562) as parents of Thomas Mercer (1524-1585).
sources.notes: Multi-site corroboration (WikiTree Mercer-2140, Geni, genealogieonline, Ancestry) for Thomas Mercer (691)'s parents. Thomas Henry Mercer b. ~1485; m. ~1498 Hawkhurst; Mary Joan Hatch b. 1498 Hawkhurst, d. 20 Jun 1562 (some sources 1548). Confidence medium-high -- compiled genealogy only.

sources.description: William Hatch + Margaret Horton as parents of Mary Joan Hatch (1498 Hawkhurst).
sources.notes: Ancestry record card for Joane de Hatch names parents as William Hatch and Margaret Horton. Single-source claim.

sources.description: John Wyatt (~1477 Braunton-1525) as father of Phillip Wyatt (~1500-1591).
sources.notes: WikiTree Wyatt-162 + Geni + Rootsweb corroborate John Wyatt b. ~1477 Braunton, d. 1525 as father of Phillip Wyatt (693). Wife not identified in compiled sources.

sources.description: Geoffrey Paty (~1470 Braunton-~1527) + Jane Braunton (1474-~1534) as parents of Joan Paty.
sources.notes: WikiTree Paty-1 and Paty-92 both name Geoffrey Paty as father. Ancestry record 'Lady Jane Braunton' names her as wife of Sir Geoffrey Paty m. ~1504 Pilton.

sources.description: John (2) Henkle (~1483 Lenham-~1523 Harrietsham) + Margaret Launcelyn as parents of John Hinckley (~1512-1577).
sources.notes: WikiTree Hynckleye-1 explicitly lists parents as John (2) Henkle and Margaret Launcelyn. Geni corroborates.

sources.description: John Whitfield (~1520 Wadhurst-1585) + Elizabeth Stacy (~1526-?) as putative parents of Maria Whitfield (1544 Hawkhurst). LOW CONFIDENCE.
sources.notes: LOW CONFIDENCE. Whitfield One-Name Study documents children Clement (1548), Margaret (1550), Bridget (1553), Rebecca (1554), John (1555), George (1556), Thomas (1557). Maria (1544) NOT in list.

sources.description: Robert Whitfield (~1453-1541) of Wadhurst, Sussex + Agnes Giles of Biddington, Kent as parents of John Whitfield I (708). Robert's father Miles Whitfield of Newtonbury, Alston Moor, Cumberland m. Matilda.
sources.location: Wadhurst, Sussex / Newtonbury, Alston Moor, Cumberland / Biddington, Kent
sources.notes: Multi-source corroboration. Whitfield One-Name Study Section 2 documents Robert's two marriages (Katherine Wenbourne, then Agnes Giles), Cumberland origin, ironmaster status at Wadhurst, age 88 at death June 1541. Robert and Agnes had three sons (Robert 1517, John 1520, Thomas 1524) and a daughter (Elizabeth 1522).

sources.description: Clement Stacey of Tenterden, Kent as father of Elizabeth Stacy (709, b. ~1526), wife of John Whitfield I.
sources.notes: Whitfield One-Name Study Section 4 names Elizabeth Stacey's father as Clement Stacey of Tenterden. No specific dates surfaced for Clement himself; medium confidence.

sources.description: Sir John Mercer (1449-c.1500) and Mary Howe (c.1445-1495) as parents of Thomas Henry Mercer (699). Hawkhurst, Kent.
sources.notes: FamilySearch PID LRLG-Z21 (Sir John Mercer); Geni 6000000043431433056 (John Mercer); Geni 6000000061007243353 (Mary Howe); WikiTree Mercer-2140 ancestral notes; Ancestry record 24-11z7st3. Multi-site corroboration. Search snippets reference TNA grant '20 Hen VII / John Mercer senior, son of Thomas Mercer senior' as additional evidence of the John-as-son-of-Thomas relationship.

sources.description: Sir Thomas Mercer (c.1420-c.1472/1489) and Hannah Taylor (c.1430-c.1480) as parents of Sir John Mercer (716). Hawkhurst, Kent.
sources.notes: FamilySearch PID LRLG-XJJ (Sir Thomas Mercer 1420-1489); also LRLG-XJJ '1420-1472' variant. Search-snippet corroboration via WikiTree Mercer-2140 ancestral notes and Ancestry/MyHeritage compiled trees. Death-year discrepancy (1472 vs 1489) carried as research lead.

sources.description: Richard Whitfield (Randalholme branch, Cumberland) as father of Miles Whitfield (712). Per the 1574 Whitfield pedigree (John Whitfield of Skelgill) and 1575 Herald's visitation by William Dethicke (York Herald), Richard had four sons: Robert, John, Myles (Miles), and Thomas. Miles was alive in 1517, almost certainly dead in 1522.
sources.notes: Whitfield One-Name Study, 'Randalholme Generations 12 to 15' page. Corroborated by Wikipedia 'Whitfield family' article and FamilyCentral/Putman Whitfield genealogy summaries. Direct egress-blocked, content captured from search-result snippets.

sources.description: Mercer family ancestry — Thomas Mercer (~1390) + Elizabeth as parents of Sir Thomas Mercer (~1420). Multi-site corroboration.
sources.notes: WikiTree Mercer-2140 ancestral notes; Geni Thomas Mercer (1524-1585) ancestor section 6000000000421526066; Ancestry record 24-3h5mhy0; theweald.org Hawkhurst Mercer surname listings. Thomas Mercer (~1390-~1450) of Hawkhurst, Kent, married Elizabeth (maiden surname unknown); their son was Sir Thomas Mercer (~1420-~1472) husband of Hannah Taylor.

sources.description: Howe family — John Howe II (~1420-1460) + Agnes Keddington as parents of Mary Howe (1445-1495).
sources.notes: FamilySearch PID GTFQ-LTH (John Howe II 1420-1460); Geni 6000000000007287220 (John Howe c.1420-1460); Geni 6000000061007243353 (Mary Howe parent section); Ancestry record 24-11z7st3. Multi-site agreement.

sources.description: Whitfield Randalholme — William Whitfield (~1410) + Maud Whitley (~1395) as ancestors of Richard Whitfield (720). Per the 1574 pedigree by John Whitfield of Skelgill (age 94) and William Vepont (70), prepared at Whitfeld Hall on 1 June 1574.
sources.notes: William Whitfield (brother or cousin of Sir Matthew Whitfield, generation 11, High Sheriff of Northumberland) acquired Randalholme c.1410 through marriage to Maud Whitley, daughter and heir of William de Whitelaw of Randalholme. WikiTree Whitfield-58 (William Whitfield abt.1410-?); WikiTree Whitley-55 (Maud Whitley abt.1395-?). Per the 1574 pedigree, Richard Whitfield (720) was their grandson — but Threlfall's extended pedigree alternatively shows William as Myles Whitfield (712)'s direct father. Linkage to Richard not yet set as father_id.

sources.description: Giles family — Elizabeth (Brudenell) Giles as wife of John Giles of Biddenden, Kent, and mother of Agnes Giles (711).
sources.notes: Whitfield One-Name Study Section 2: Agnes 'the daughter and sole heir of John Giles of Biddenden in Kent.' Brudenell wife reported on Ancestry record 24-2qnf3tk (Annie Agnes Gibbons-Giles 1475-1567 parent section); corroborated by webtrees myfamilytree.scot. Note: location should be Biddenden (not Biddington as previously recorded).

sources.description: Hinckley family — John Hynckleye I (~1455-1484) of Lenham/Harrietsham, Kent + Margaret (Unknown) as parents of John (2) Henkle (706).
sources.notes: WikiTree Hynckleye-31 (John Hynckleye I abt.1455-5 Jan 1484); confirms his father was John Henclyf (b. ~1430 — research lead). Children John Henkle II (706) and Robert Hynckleye. Surname variants Henkele/Henkle/Hynckleye/Hinckley. Died 5 January 1484 at Harrietsham just months after the birth of his son John (2) Henkle (706, ~1483).

sources.description: John Henclyf the Elder of Tenham, Kent, will dated 1463/64; left land in Mellefeld and 'Johnthomas gardyn' / 'Lytyl-garden' to his sons to glaze the long window on the north head of Tenham Church (still known as 'The Hinkley Chancel'). Wife Jane. Father of John Henkele who left a 1484 will to wife Marjorie and sons John, Robert, Thomas (= our John Hynckleye I 728).
sources.notes: British History Online 'Parishes: Tenham' (Hasted, History and Topographical Survey of Kent vol 6); Geni profiles John 'The Elder' Hinckley/Hinclyne/Henclive of Henhann (6000000007184868537) and John Hinckley/Hinkle/Henkle 1410-1483 (6000000000421523980); WikiTree Hynckleye-31. Note: dates in compiled trees vary (Geni gives 1375 for elder; Hasted's 1463 will date is the primary anchor). Used b. ~1410, d. 1463 to fit chronology of son b. ~1455.

sources.description: John Howe (c.1380 - c.1450) of Hawkhurst, Kent. Wife 'Lady Bette Howe' (per Geni profile). Father of John Howe II (c.1420-1460), husband of Agnes Keddington. Geni 6000000020533533022.
sources.notes: Single Geni source; chronology fits John Howe II (b. ~1420, d. ~1460). Wife 'Lady Bette' is single-source and the 'Lady' honorific is unverified -- not added this session.

sources.description: William de Whitelaw (alias William Whitley) of Randalholme, Cumberland: father of Maud Whitley (726). 'William died leaving three daughters, one of whom was Maud, who became the wife of William Whitfield.' His wife was a granddaughter of Robert de Vipont/Vetripont (d. 13 Jul 1371) -- one of two granddaughters who inherited the Vipont Cumberland estates. Maud's portion was the manor house of Randalholme, lands in Aldstonmoor and Kirkhaugh.
sources.notes: Whitfield One-Name Study 'Randalholme Generations 12 to 15'; WikiTree Whitley-55 (Maud's profile); WikiTree Vipont-25 (Robert de Vipont c.1303-1371); 1570s witness-testimony quote on John Whetlay/Whitley as sole seized of Randalholme; Hodgson's History of Northumberland references the post-mortem partition. Note the 1570s testimony uses 'John Whetlay or Whitley' rather than 'William' -- the One-Name Study and 1956 article reconcile by giving William as the form used in compiled genealogies.

sources.description: Hinckley/Hinclyne line at Lenham/Tenham, Kent, generations c.1300-c.1375. John 'The Elder' Hinckley/Hinclyne/Henclive (730) is supported on Geni profile https://www.geni.com/people/John-The-Elder-Hinckley/6000000007184868537 with father John Hinckley/Hinclyne/Henclive (b. 1350) profile https://www.geni.com/people/John-Hinckley-Hinclyne-Henclive/6000000003215278431 and grandfather John Hinckley/Hinclyne, of Lenham (b. c.1300) profile https://www.geni.com/people/John-Hinckley-Hinclyne-of-Lenham/6000000042520391316 . Primary anchor: 'A John de Henclyne of Tenham, Kent, member of the Cinque Ports, assessed for Knighting the Black Prince in 1346, paid 20/- in 1348 for the farm of Gore & Pete and 16/- for the rent of Mellefeld (Tenham) to the steward of the Archbishop of Canterbury' -- this 1346/1348 individual is the c.1300-born John of Lenham. Cross-checked with British History Online 'Parishes: Tenham' (Hasted vol 6) for Mellefeld and Hinkley Chancel context.
sources.date: 1346 (Black Prince Knighting Aid); 1463 (will of John 'The Elder')
sources.notes: Geni places John 'The Elder' (1375-1463) as a generation prior to the John (1410-1483) who made the 1484 will. Our existing 730 has b. ~1410, which conflicts with Geni's 1375 birth year for The Elder. The existing tree may collapse The Elder + his son John (1410-1483) into a single individual (730). For this session, we add the parents of 730 per Geni without altering 730's existing dates -- a future session should verify whether 730 represents one person or two. WikiTree Hynckleye-31 simply lists 728's father as 'John Henclyf' without a generation designation, supporting either reading.

sources.description: Lady Bette Howe (De Huff), wife of John Howe (732) at Hawkhurst, Kent. Geni profile https://www.geni.com/people/Lady-Bette-Howe/6000000055012025046 lists her with maiden name 'De Huff' and birth c.1380.
sources.notes: Single-source (Geni). The 'Lady' honorific is unverified in primary sources -- we use 'Bette' without the title here. Maiden surname 'De Huff' is uncommon and may be a compiled-tree artifact. LOW confidence.

sources.description: Joan (Vipont) de Whitlawe, wife of William de Whitelaw (733). WikiTree Vipont-28 https://www.wikitree.com/wiki/Vipont-28 : Joan b. before 1346, daughter of Nicholas de Vipont (1320-1362) and Elena (Aubeney) de Vipont (~1327-1362, WikiTree Aubeney-3 https://www.wikitree.com/wiki/Aubeney-3 ); sister Elizabeth (Vipont) de Blencowe; brother Robert (held manor of Johnby, d. 1371). Joan's grandfather was Robert de Vipont (1303-1371, WikiTree Vipont-25 https://www.wikitree.com/wiki/Vipont-25 ) -- Robert outlived his eldest son Nicholas, leaving Joan and Elizabeth as direct heiresses of the Vipont/Vetripont Cumberland estates in 1371. One inherited Randalholme (the Whitelaw share); their daughter Maud Whitley (726) carried that share into the Whitfield line. Joan's marriage to William de Whitlawe is also noted in the 1956 article cited by the Whitfield One-Name Study Randalholme Generations 12-15 page.
sources.notes: Resolves the prior session's open question of whether Robert de Vipont's heiress was Elizabeth or Joan. WikiTree Vipont-28 is unambiguous: Joan married William de Whitlawe; Elizabeth married Thomas de Blencowe. Robert de Vipont (Vipont-25) is the grandfather; we add Joan, her father Nicholas (738), and her mother Elena Aubeney (739) to the tree this session, leaving Robert as a research lead for one further generation back.
